Abstract: As artificial intelligence shifts from pure tool for delegation toward agentic collaboration, its use in the arts can shift beyond the exploration of machine autonomy toward synergistic co-creation. While our earlier robotic works utilized automation to distance the artist's intent from the final mark, we present Companion: an artistic apparatus that integrates a drawing robot with Large Language Models (LLMs) to re-center human-machine presence. By leveraging in-context learning and real-time tool use, the system engages in bidirectional interaction via speech and sketching. This approach transforms the robot from a passive executor into a playful co-creative partner capable of driving shared visual storytelling into unexpected aesthetic territories. To validate this collaborative shift, we employed the Consensual Assessment Technique (CAT) with a panel of seven art-world experts. Results confirm that the system produces works with a distinct aesthetic identity and professional exhibition merit, demonstrating the potential of AI as a highly capable artistic collaborator.

zh.title: 一个用于视觉叙事的具身伴侣
zh.summary: Companion 整合绘画机器人和大语言模型，通过语音和素描交互实现双向人工智能共创协作。专家评估证实该系统产生了具有美学特色且具有专业展览价值的作品，证明了人工智能作为协作艺术伙伴的潜力。

fr.title: Un Compagnon Incarné pour la Narration Visuelle
fr.summary: Companion intègre un robot de dessin avec les modèles de langage pour permettre une collaboration créative bidirectionnelle homme-IA grâce aux interactions vocales et au dessin. L'évaluation d'experts confirme que le système produit des œuvres esthétiquement distinctes ayant un mérite d'exposition professionnelle, démontrant le potentiel de l'IA en tant que partenaire artistique collaboratif.

de.title: Ein Verkörperter Begleiter für Visuelles Storytelling
de.summary: Companion integriert einen Zeichenroboter mit großen Sprachmodellen, um bidirektionale kreative Zusammenarbeit zwischen Mensch und KI durch Sprach- und Skizzeninteraktionen zu ermöglichen. Fachbewertungen bestätigen, dass das System ästhetisch unterschiedliche Werke mit professionellem Ausstellungsverdienst produziert und das Potenzial der KI als kollaborativer künstlerischer Partner demonstriert.

es.title: Un Compañero Encarnado para la Narración Visual
es.summary: Companion integra un robot de dibujo con modelos de lenguaje grande para permitir la colaboración creativa bidireccional hombre-IA a través de interacciones de voz y bocetos. La evaluación de expertos confirma que el sistema produce obras estéticamente distintas con mérito de exposición profesional, demostrando el potencial de la IA como socio artístico colaborativo.

title: From Toil to Thought: Designing for Strategic Exploration and Responsible AI in Systematic Literature Reviews
summary: This paper identifies friction points in systematic literature reviews and presents ARC, a system that integrates multi-database search, transparent iterative querying, and AI-assisted screening. The system helps researchers transition from managing administrative overhead to strategic exploration while maintaining scholarly judgment and agency.
content:
arXiv:2603.05514v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Systematic Literature Reviews (SLRs) are fundamental to scientific progress, yet the process is hindered by a fragmented tool ecosystem that imposes a high cognitive load. This friction suppresses the iterative, exploratory nature of scholarly work. To investigate these challenges, we conducted an exploratory design study with 20 experienced researchers. This study identified key friction points: 1) the high cognitive load of managing iterative query refinement across multiple databases, 2) the overwhelming scale and pace of publication of modern literature, and 3) the tension between automation and scholarly agency.
  Informed by these findings, we developed ARC, a design probe that operationalizes solutions for multi-database integration, transparent iterative search, and verifiable AI-assisted screening. A comparative user study with 8 researchers suggests that an integrated environment facilitates a transition in scholarly work, moving researchers from managing administrative overhead to engaging in strategic exploration. By utilizing external representations to scaffold strategic exploration and transparent AI reasoning, our system supports verifiable judgment, aiming to augment expert contributions from initial creation through long-term maintenance of knowledge synthesis.

zh.title: 从繁琐到思考：为系统文献评审中的战略探索和负责任人工智能设计
zh.summary: 本文识别了系统文献评审中的摩擦点，并介绍了ARC系统，该系统集成了多数据库搜索、透明迭代查询和人工智能辅助筛选。该系统帮助研究人员从管理行政负担转向战略探索，同时保持学术判断力和自主权。

fr.title: De la Peine à la Pensée : Concevoir l'Exploration Stratégique et l'IA Responsable dans les Revues Systématiques de la Littérature
fr.summary: Cet article identifie les points de friction dans les revues systématiques de la littérature et présente ARC, un système qui intègre la recherche multibases de données, l'interrogation itérative transparente et le criblage assisté par l'IA. Le système aide les chercheurs à passer de la gestion des frais généraux administratifs à l'exploration stratégique tout en préservant le jugement académique et l'autonomie.

de.title: Von Mühsal zu Gedanke: Gestaltung für strategische Exploration und verantwortungsvolle KI in systematischen Literaturübersichten
de.summary: Dieses Papier identifiziert Reibungspunkte bei systematischen Literaturübersichten und präsentiert ARC, ein System, das Mehrfachdatenbanksuche, transparente iterative Abfragen und KI-gestützte Screening integriert. Das System hilft Forschern, vom Umgang mit administrativem Overhead zur strategischen Exploration überzugehen und dabei wissenschaftliches Urteilsvermögen und Handlungsfähigkeit zu bewahren.

es.title: De la Penuria al Pensamiento: Diseño para la Exploración Estratégica e IA Responsable en Revisiones Sistemáticas de la Literatura
es.summary: Este artículo identifica puntos de fricción en revisiones sistemáticas de la literatura y presenta ARC, un sistema que integra búsqueda multibases de datos, consultas iterativas transparentes y cribado asistido por IA. El sistema ayuda a los investigadores a pasar de la gestión de la sobrecarga administrativa a la exploración estratégica mientras mantienen el juicio académico y la agencia.

title: Traversal-as-Policy: Log-Distilled Gated Behavior Trees as Externalized, Verifiable Policies for Safe, Robust, and Efficient Agents
summary: The paper proposes distilling LLM agent execution logs into Gated Behavior Trees (GBT) that treat tree traversal as the explicit control policy with pre-execution safety gates. On SWE-bench Verified, GBT achieves 73.6% success (up from 34.6%), reduces violations to 0.2%, and cuts token usage by 39% while matching long-horizon baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.05517v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Autonomous LLM agents fail because long-horizon policy remains implicit in model weights and transcripts, while safety is retrofitted post hoc. We propose Traversal-as-Policy: distill sandboxed OpenHands execution logs into a single executable Gated Behavior Tree (GBT) and treat tree traversal -- rather than unconstrained generation -- as the control policy whenever a task is in coverage. Each node encodes a state-conditioned action macro mined and merge-checked from successful trajectories; macros implicated by unsafe traces attach deterministic pre-execution gates over structured tool context and bounded history, updated under experience-grounded monotonicity so previously rejected unsafe contexts cannot be re-admitted. At runtime, a lightweight traverser matches the base model's intent to child macros, executes one macro at a time under global and node-local gating, and when stalled performs risk-aware shortest-path recovery to a feasible success leaf; the visited path forms a compact spine memory that replaces transcript replay. Evaluated in a unified OpenHands sandbox on 15+ software, web, reasoning, and safety/security benchmarks, GBT improves success while driving violations toward zero and reducing cost. On SWE-bench Verified (Protocol A, 500 issues), GBT-SE raises success from 34.6% to 73.6%, reduces violations from 2.8% to 0.2%, and cuts token/character usage from 208k/820k to 126k/490k; with the same distilled tree, 8B executors more than double success on SWE-bench Verified (14.0%58.8%) and WebArena (9.1%37.3%).

zh.title: 遍历作为策略：日志蒸馏的门控行为树作为安全、健壮和高效代理的外部化、可验证策略
zh.summary: 该论文提议将大语言模型代理执行日志蒸馏为门控行为树，将树遍历视为具有执行前安全门的显式控制策略。在SWE-bench Verified上，该方法达到73.6%的成功率（从34.6%提高），将违规降至0.2%，并在与长时间基线匹配的同时减少39%的令牌使用。

fr.title: Traversée-comme-Politique : Arbres de Comportement Contrôlés Distillés des Journaux comme Politiques Externalisées et Vérifiables pour des Agents Sûrs, Robustes et Efficaces
fr.summary: L'article propose de distiller les journaux d'exécution d'agents LLM en arbres de comportement contrôlés qui traitent la traversée d'arbres comme la politique de contrôle explicite avec des portes de sécurité pré-exécution. Sur SWE-bench Verified, cette approche atteint un succès de 73,6% (en hausse de 34,6%), réduit les violations à 0,2% et réduit l'utilisation de tokens de 39% tout en égalant les références long-horizon.

de.title: Traversierung als Richtlinie: Aus Protokollen destillierte gesteuerte Verhaltensbäume als externalisierte, überprüfbare Richtlinien für sichere, robuste und effiziente Agenten
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt vor, Ausführungsprotokolle von LLM-Agenten in gesteuerte Verhaltensbäume zu destillieren, die Baumtraversierung als explizite Kontrollrichtlinie mit Pre-Execution-Sicherheitstoren behandeln. Bei SWE-bench Verified erreicht dieser Ansatz eine Erfolgsquote von 73,6% (gegenüber 34,6%), reduziert Verstöße auf 0,2% und senkt die Token-Nutzung um 39%, während er mit Baseline-Modellen auf lange Sicht mitkommt.

es.title: Traversal-como-Política: Árboles de Comportamiento Controlados Destilados de Registros como Políticas Externas y Verificables para Agentes Seguros, Robustos y Eficientes
es.summary: El artículo propone destilar los registros de ejecución de agentes LLM en Árboles de Comportamiento Controlados que tratan la traversal del árbol como la política de control explícita con puertas de seguridad pre-ejecución. En SWE-bench Verified, este enfoque logra un éxito del 73.6% (desde 34.6%), reduce las violaciones al 0.2% y reduce el uso de tokens en un 39% mientras iguala las líneas base a largo plazo.

title: Molecular Representations for AI in Chemistry and Materials Science: An NLP Perspective
summary: This survey reviews digital molecular representations inspired by NLP techniques used in chemistry and materials science applications. It provides a reference guide for researchers with limited chemical representation experience who want to apply AI methods at the intersection of these fields.
content:
arXiv:2603.05525v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Deep learning, a subfield of machine learning, has gained importance in various application areas in recent years. Its growing popularity has led it to enter the natural sciences as well. This has created the need for molecular representations that are both machine-readable and understandable to scientists from different fields. Over the years, many chemical molecular representations have been constructed, and new ones continue to be developed as computer technology advances and knowledge of molecular complexity increases. This paper presents some of the most popular digital molecular representations inspired by natural language processing (NLP) and used in chemical informatics. In addition, the paper discusses some notable AI-based applications that use these representations. This paper aims to provide a guide to structural representations that are important for the application of AI in chemistry and materials science from the perspective of an NLP researcher. This review is a reference tool for researchers with little experience working with chemical representations who wish to work on projects at the interface of these fields.

zh.title: 分子表示在化学和材料科学中的AI应用：自然语言处理视角
zh.summary: 本综述回顾了受化学和材料科学应用中NLP技术启发的数字分子表示方法。它为具有有限化学表示经验且希望在这些领域交叉应用AI方法的研究人员提供参考指南。

fr.title: Représentations Moléculaires pour l'IA en Chimie et Science des Matériaux : Une Perspective du Traitement du Langage Naturel
fr.summary: Cet aperçu examine les représentations moléculaires numériques inspirées par les techniques de traitement du langage naturel utilisées dans les applications de chimie et de science des matériaux. Il fournit un guide de référence pour les chercheurs ayant une expérience limitée en représentation chimique qui souhaitent appliquer des méthodes d'IA à l'intersection de ces domaines.

de.title: Molekulare Repräsentationen für KI in Chemie und Materialwissenschaften: Eine NLP-Perspektive
de.summary: Diese Übersicht überprüft digitale molekulare Repräsentationen, die von NLP-Techniken inspiriert sind, die in Chemie- und Materialwissenschaftsanwendungen verwendet werden. Sie bietet einen Referenzleitfaden für Forscher mit begrenzter Erfahrung in chemischen Repräsentationen, die KI-Methoden an der Schnittstelle dieser Felder anwenden möchten.

es.title: Representaciones Moleculares para IA en Química y Ciencia de Materiales: Una Perspectiva del Procesamiento del Lenguaje Natural
es.summary: Esta revisión examina representaciones moleculares digitales inspiradas en técnicas de procesamiento del lenguaje natural utilizadas en aplicaciones de química y ciencia de materiales. Proporciona una guía de referencia para investigadores con experiencia limitada en representación química que desean aplicar métodos de IA en la intersección de estos campos.

title: Omni-C: Compressing Heterogeneous Modalities into a Single Dense Encoder
summary: Omni-C proposes a single dense Transformer encoder that learns shared representations across images, audio, and text through contrastive pretraining, eliminating the complexity of separate expert encoders. The approach achieves comparable performance to expert models while substantially reducing inference memory usage and supporting efficient deployment on memory-constrained systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.05528v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Recent multimodal systems often rely on separate expert modality encoders which cause linearly scaling complexity and computational overhead with added modalities. While unified Omni-models address this via Mixture-of-Expert (MoE) architectures with specialized experts and routing, they still inflate parameter counts and introduce routing overhead. In this paper, we propose Omni-C (Omni-Compress), a single dense Transformer-based encoder that learns competitive shared representations across heterogeneous modalities--images, audio, and text--through unimodal contrastive pretraining on large-scale unaligned data. By maximizing parameter sharing in the backbone and using lightweight modality-specific projection heads, Omni-C effectively mitigates inter-modality conflicts without requiring MoE, paired supervision, or routing. This design supports efficient deployment on memory-constrained systems via sequential modality processing and low-memory inference, eliminating the need for parallel expert loading or specialized hardware. Experiments show Omni-C achieves performance comparable to expert models in unimodal and cross-model tasks, with modest zero-shot degradation on audio and text that is largely recovered through lightweight linear probing or parameter efficient fine-tuning. The unified architecture substantially reduces inference memory usage compared to multi-encoder baselines, advancing efficient and scalable multimodal learning.

zh.title: Omni-C：将异构模态压缩为单个密集编码器
zh.summary: Omni-C提出了一个单一的密集Transformer编码器，通过对比预训练学习跨图像、音频和文本的共享表示，消除了独立专家编码器的复杂性。该方法实现了与专家模型相当的性能，同时大幅降低推理内存使用量并支持在内存受限的系统上高效部署。

fr.title: Omni-C : Compression des Modalités Hétérogènes dans un Seul Encodeur Dense
fr.summary: Omni-C propose un seul encodeur Transformer dense qui apprend des représentations partagées entre les images, l'audio et le texte par préentraînement contrastif, éliminant la complexité des encodeurs experts séparés. L'approche atteint des performances comparables aux modèles experts tout en réduisant considérablement l'utilisation mémoire lors de l'inférence et en prenant en charge un déploiement efficace sur les systèmes à mémoire limitée.

de.title: Omni-C: Komprimierung heterogener Modalitäten in einem einzelnen dichten Encoder
de.summary: Omni-C schlägt einen einzelnen dichten Transformer-Encoder vor, der durch kontrastives Vortraining gemeinsame Darstellungen über Bilder, Audio und Text erlernt und die Komplexität separater Experten-Encoder eliminiert. Der Ansatz erreicht eine vergleichbare Leistung mit Expertenmodellen und reduziert gleichzeitig die Speichernutzung bei der Inferenz erheblich, während eine effiziente Bereitstellung auf speicherbegrenzten Systemen unterstützt wird.

es.title: Omni-C: Compresión de Modalidades Heterogéneas en un Único Codificador Denso
es.summary: Omni-C propone un codificador Transformer denso único que aprende representaciones compartidas entre imágenes, audio y texto a través del preentrenamiento contrastivo, eliminando la complejidad de codificadores expertos separados. El enfoque logra un rendimiento comparable a los modelos expertos mientras reduce sustancialmente el uso de memoria en la inferencia y admite una implementación eficiente en sistemas con memoria limitada.

title: Towards Neural Graph Data Management
summary: This paper introduces NGDBench, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating neural AI systems on graph database tasks across finance, medicine, and other domains using the full Cypher query language. Evaluation reveals significant limitations in LLMs and RAG methods for structured reasoning, establishing a critical testbed for advancing neural graph capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.05529v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: While AI systems have made remarkable progress in processing unstructured text, structured data such as graphs stored in databases, continues to grow rapidly yet remains difficult for neural models to effectively utilize. We introduce NGDBench, a unified benchmark for evaluating neural graph database capabilities across five diverse domains, including finance, medicine, and AI agent tooling. Unlike prior benchmarks limited to elementary logical operations, NGDBench supports the full Cypher query language, enabling complex pattern matching, variable-length paths, and numerical aggregations, while incorporating realistic noise injection and dynamic data management operations. Our evaluation of state-of-the-art LLMs and RAG methods reveals significant limitations in structured reasoning, noise robustness, and analytical precision, establishing NGDBench as a critical testbed for advancing neural graph data management. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/HKUST-KnowComp/NGDBench.

zh.title: 走向神经图形数据管理
zh.summary: 本论文介绍了NGDBench，这是一个全面的基准，用于评估神经AI系统在使用完整Cypher查询语言的图形数据库任务中的性能，涵盖金融、医学等多个领域。评估揭示了LLMs和RAG方法在结构化推理中的重大局限性，为推进神经图形能力建立了关键的测试平台。

fr.title: Vers la Gestion Neurale des Données Graphiques
fr.summary: Cet article introduit NGDBench, un benchmark complet pour évaluer les systèmes d'IA neurale sur les tâches de base de données graphiques dans la finance, la médecine et d'autres domaines en utilisant le langage de requête Cypher complet. L'évaluation révèle des limitations importantes des LLMs et des méthodes RAG pour le raisonnement structuré, établissant un banc d'essai critique pour faire progresser les capacités graphiques neuronales.

de.title: Auf dem Weg zu neuronaler Graphdatenverwaltung
de.summary: Dieses Papier führt NGDBench ein, eine umfassende Benchmark zur Bewertung neuronaler KI-Systeme auf Graphdatenbank-Aufgaben in Finanzen, Medizin und anderen Bereichen mit der vollständigen Cypher-Abfragesprache. Die Auswertung zeigt erhebliche Einschränkungen bei LLMs und RAG-Methoden für strukturiertes Denken und etabliert einen kritischen Testbench zur Förderung neuronaler Graphfähigkeiten.

es.title: Hacia la Gestión Neuronal de Datos de Gráficos
es.summary: Este artículo introduce NGDBench, un punto de referencia integral para evaluar sistemas de IA neuronal en tareas de bases de datos gráficas en finanzas, medicina y otros dominios utilizando el lenguaje de consultas Cypher completo. La evaluación revela limitaciones significativas en LLMs y métodos RAG para el razonamiento estructurado, estableciendo una plataforma de prueba crítica para avanzar en las capacidades de gráficos neurales.

title: On the Reliability of AI Methods in Drug Discovery: Evaluation of Boltz-2 for Structure and Binding Affinity Prediction
summary: This evaluation of Boltz-2, a biomolecular foundation model, reveals it lacks the energetic resolution needed for lead identification despite offering speed for initial screening compared to physics-based methods. The findings highlight the necessity of combining AI efficiency with physics-based precision for reliable drug discovery applications.
content:
arXiv:2603.05532v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Despite continuing hype about the role of AI in drug discovery, no "AI-discovered drugs" have so far received regulatory approval. Here we assess one of the latest AI based tools in this domain. The ability to rapidly predict protein-ligand structures and binding affinities is pivotal for accelerating drug discovery. Boltz-2, a recently developed biomolecular foundation model, aims to bridge the gap between AI efficiency and physics-based precision through a joint "co-folding" approach. In this study, we provide an extensive evaluation of Boltz-2 using two large-scale datasets: 16,780 compounds for 3CLPro and 21,702 compounds for TNKS2. We compare Boltz-2 predicted structures with traditional docking and binding affinities with binding free energies derived from the physics-based ESMACS protocol. Structural analysis reveals significant global RMSD variations, indicating that Boltz-2 predicts multiple protein conformations and ligand binding positions rather than a single converged pose. Energetic evaluations exhibit only weak to moderate correlations across the global datasets. Furthermore, a focused analysis of the top 100 compounds yields no significant correlation between the Boltz-2 predictions and the binding free energies from fine-grained ESMACS, alongside observed saturation difference in ligand structures. Our results show that while Boltz-2 offers substantial speed for initial screening, it lacks the energetic resolution required for lead identification. These findings highlight the necessity of employing physics-based methods for the reliability and refinement of AI-derived models.

zh.title: 论AI方法在药物发现中的可靠性：Boltz-2结构与结合亲和力预测的评估
zh.summary: 对生物分子基础模型Boltz-2的评估表明，尽管相比物理方法具有初期筛选速度优势，但它缺乏先导物识别所需的能量分辨率。研究结果突出了在可靠的药物发现应用中结合AI效率与物理精确性的必要性。

fr.title: Sur la fiabilité des méthodes d'IA dans la découverte de médicaments : Évaluation de Boltz-2 pour la prédiction de structure et d'affinité de liaison
fr.summary: Cette évaluation de Boltz-2, un modèle fondationnel biomolécculaire, révèle qu'il manque de la résolution énergétique nécessaire pour l'identification des composés principaux malgré sa rapidité pour le criblage initial comparée aux méthodes basées sur la physique. Les résultats mettent en évidence la nécessité de combiner l'efficacité de l'IA avec la précision basée sur la physique pour des applications fiables de découverte de médicaments.

de.title: Zur Zuverlässigkeit von KI-Methoden in der Wirkstoffentdeckung: Bewertung von Boltz-2 für Struktur- und Bindungsaffinitätsvorhersage
de.summary: Diese Bewertung von Boltz-2, einem biomolekularen Grundlagenmodell, zeigt, dass es der für die Leitverbindungsidentifizierung erforderlichen Energieauflösung entbehrt, obwohl es für das anfängliche Screening schneller ist als physikbasierte Methoden. Die Ergebnisse unterstreichen die Notwendigkeit, KI-Effizienz mit physikbasierter Präzision für zuverlässige Anwendungen der Wirkstoffentdeckung zu verbinden.

es.title: Sobre la confiabilidad de los métodos de IA en el descubrimiento de fármacos: Evaluación de Boltz-2 para la predicción de estructura y afinidad de unión
es.summary: Esta evaluación de Boltz-2, un modelo fundamental biomolecular, revela que carece de la resolución energética necesaria para la identificación de compuestos líderes a pesar de ofrecer rapidez en el cribado inicial en comparación con métodos basados en física. Los hallazgos destacan la necesidad de combinar la eficiencia de la IA con la precisión basada en física para aplicaciones confiables de descubrimiento de fármacos.

title: JAWS: Enhancing Long-term Rollout of Neural Operators via Spatially-Adaptive Jacobian Regularization
summary: JAWS introduces a probabilistic regularization strategy that adaptively modulates constraint strength based on local physical complexity to improve long-term stability in neural surrogate models. The approach enables shorter-horizon trajectory optimization to match or exceed long-horizon baselines while reducing computational costs and improving shock fidelity.
content:
arXiv:2603.05538v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Data-driven surrogate models improve the efficiency of simulating continuous dynamical systems, yet their autoregressive rollouts are often limited by instability and spectral blow-up. While global regularization techniques can enforce contractive dynamics, they uniformly damp high-frequency features, introducing a contraction-dissipation dilemma. Furthermore, long-horizon trajectory optimization methods that explicitly correct drift are bottlenecked by memory constraints. In this work, we propose Jacobian-Adaptive Weighting for Stability (JAWS), a probabilistic regularization strategy designed to mitigate these limitations. By framing operator learning as Maximum A Posteriori (MAP) estimation with spatially heteroscedastic uncertainty, JAWS dynamically modulates the regularization strength based on local physical complexity. This allows the model to enforce contraction in smooth regions to suppress noise, while relaxing constraints near singular features to preserve gradients, effectively realizing a behavior similar to numerical shock-capturing schemes. Experiments demonstrate that this spatially-adaptive prior serves as an effective spectral pre-conditioner, which reduces the base operator's burden of handling high-frequency instabilities. This reduction enables memory-efficient, short-horizon trajectory optimization to match or exceed the long-term accuracy of long-horizon baselines. Evaluated on the 1D viscous Burgers' equation, our hybrid approach improves long-term stability, shock fidelity, and out-of-distribution generalization while reducing training computational costs.

zh.title: JAWS：通过空间自适应雅可比正则化增强神经算子的长期展开
zh.summary: JAWS引入了一种概率正则化策略，根据局部物理复杂性自适应地调节约束强度，以改善神经代理模型的长期稳定性。该方法使得更短视距的轨迹优化能够匹配或超过长视距基线，同时降低计算成本并改进冲击保真度。

fr.title: JAWS : Améliorer le déploiement à long terme des opérateurs neuronaux via la régularisation jacobienne adaptatrice spatiale
fr.summary: JAWS introduit une stratégie de régularisation probabiliste qui module adaptatativement la force des contraintes en fonction de la complexité physique locale pour améliorer la stabilité à long terme dans les modèles de substitution neuronaux. L'approche permet une optimisation de trajectoire à horizon plus court pour égaler ou dépasser les lignes de base à long horizon tout en réduisant les coûts de calcul et en améliorant la fidélité aux chocs.

de.title: JAWS: Verbesserung des Langzeit-Rollouts von neuronalen Operatoren durch räumlich adaptive Jacobi-Regularisierung
de.summary: JAWS führt eine probabilistische Regularisierungsstrategie ein, die die Einschränkungsstärke basierend auf lokaler physikalischer Komplexität adaptiv moduliert, um die Langzeitstabilität in neuronalen Surrogatmodellen zu verbessern. Der Ansatz ermöglicht eine kürzere Horizont-Trajektorienoptimierung, um langfristige Baselines zu erfüllen oder zu übertreffen, während gleichzeitig Rechenkosten gesenkt und die Shock-Treue verbessert werden.

es.title: JAWS: Mejoramiento del despliegue a largo plazo de operadores neuronales mediante regularización jacobiana adaptativamente espacial
es.summary: JAWS introduce una estrategia de regularización probabilística que modula adaptativamente la fuerza de restricción basándose en la complejidad física local para mejorar la estabilidad a largo plazo en modelos sustitutos neuronales. El enfoque permite una optimización de trayectoria de horizonte más corto para igualar o superar las líneas base de horizonte largo mientras reduce los costos computacionales y mejora la fidelidad de impacto.

title: VDCook:DIY video data cook your MLLMs
summary: VDCook is a self-evolving video data operating system enabling users to request specialized training datasets through natural language queries, combining real video retrieval with controlled synthesis. The platform supports continuous dataset updates through automated ingestion and provides multi-dimensional metadata annotation for flexible downstream usage.
content:
arXiv:2603.05539v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We introduce VDCook: a self-evolving video data operating system, a configurable video data construction platform for researchers and vertical domain teams. Users initiate data requests via natural language queries and adjustable parameters (scale, retrieval-synthesis ratio, quality threshold). The system automatically performs query optimization, concurrently running real video retrieval and controlled synthesis modules. It ultimately generates in-domain data packages with complete provenance and metadata, along with reproducible Notebooks.
  Unlike traditional static, one-time-built datasets, VDCook enables continuous updates and domain expansion through its automated data ingestion mechanism based on MCP (Model Context Protocol)\cite{mcp2024anthropic}, transforming datasets into dynamically evolving open ecosystems. The system also provides multi-dimensional metadata annotation (scene segmentation, motion scoring, OCR ratio, automatic captioning, etc.), laying the foundation for flexible subsequent data `cooking' and indexing\cite{vlogger}.
  This platform aims to significantly lower the barrier to constructing specialized video training datasets through infrastructure-level solutions, while supporting community contributions and a governance-enabled data expansion paradigm. \textbf{Project demo:} https://screenapp.io/app/v/WP0SvffgsH

zh.title: VDCook：DIY视频数据为您的多模态大语言模型服务
zh.summary: VDCook是一个自进化的视频数据操作系统，使用户能够通过自然语言查询请求专业化的训练数据集，结合真实视频检索和受控合成。该平台支持通过自动摄取进行持续的数据集更新，并提供多维度元数据标注以灵活支持下游使用。

fr.title: VDCook : Préparez vos données vidéo DIY pour vos MLLM
fr.summary: VDCook est un système d'exploitation de données vidéo auto-évolutif permettant aux utilisateurs de demander des ensembles de données d'entraînement spécialisés par le biais de requêtes en langage naturel, combinant la récupération de vidéos réelles avec la synthèse contrôlée. La plateforme prend en charge les mises à jour continues des ensembles de données grâce à l'ingestion automatisée et fournit des annotations de métadonnées multidimensionnelles pour une utilisation flexible en aval.

de.title: VDCook: DIY Videodaten für Ihre MLLMs
de.summary: VDCook ist ein sich selbst weiterentwickelndes Videodaten-Betriebssystem, das Benutzern ermöglicht, spezialisierte Trainingsdatensätze durch natürlichsprachige Abfragen anzufordern, wobei echte Videobeschaffung mit kontrollierter Synthese kombiniert wird. Die Plattform unterstützt kontinuierliche Datensatz-Updates durch automatisierte Erfassung und bietet mehrdimensionale Metadaten-Annotationen für flexible nachgelagerte Verwendung.

es.title: VDCook: Prepara tus datos de video DIY para tus MLLM
es.summary: VDCook es un sistema operativo de datos de video que se auto-evoluciona, permitiendo a los usuarios solicitar conjuntos de datos de capacitación especializados mediante consultas de lenguaje natural, combinando recuperación de video real con síntesis controlada. La plataforma admite actualizaciones continuas de conjuntos de datos mediante ingesta automatizada y proporciona anotaciones de metadatos multidimensionales para uso flexible en aplicaciones posteriores.

title: Human-Data Interaction, Exploration, and Visualization in the AI Era: Challenges and Opportunities
summary: This paper investigates challenges in human-centered data analysis systems introduced by AI advances, including latency misalignment, scalability constraints, and uncertainty from foundation models. It calls for redefining human-machine roles and incorporating cognitive and perceptual principles into interactive data analysis systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.05542v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The rapid advancement of AI is transforming human-centered systems, with profound implications for human-AI interaction, human-data interaction, and visual analytics. In the AI era, data analysis increasingly involves large-scale, heterogeneous, and multimodal data that is predominantly unstructured, as well as foundation models such as LLMs and VLMs, which introduce additional uncertainty into analytical processes. These shifts expose persistent challenges for human-data interactive systems, including perceptually misaligned latency, scalability constraints, limitations of existing interaction and exploration paradigms, and growing uncertainty regarding the reliability and interpretability of AI-generated insights. Responding to these challenges requires moving beyond conventional efficiency and scalability metrics, redefining the roles of humans and machines in analytical workflows, and incorporating cognitive, perceptual, and design principles into every level of the human-data interaction stack. This paper investigates the challenges introduced by recent advances in AI and examines how these developments are reshaping the ways users engage with data, while outlining limitations and open research directions for building human-centered AI systems for interactive data analysis in the AI era.

zh.title: 人工智能时代的人类-数据交互、探索和可视化：挑战与机遇
zh.summary: 本文研究了人工智能进步为人类中心数据分析系统带来的挑战，包括延迟错配、可扩展性限制和基础模型的不确定性。它呼吁重新定义人机角色，并将认知和感知原则融入交互式数据分析系统。

fr.title: Interaction, Exploration et Visualisation Homme-Données à l'Ère de l'IA : Défis et Opportunités
fr.summary: Cet article enquête sur les défis des systèmes d'analyse de données centrés sur l'homme introduits par les avancées en IA, notamment le désalignement de latence, les contraintes d'évolutivité et l'incertitude des modèles de fondation. Il appelle à redéfinir les rôles homme-machine et à incorporer des principes cognitifs et perceptifs dans les systèmes d'analyse de données interactifs.

de.title: Mensch-Daten-Interaktion, Exploration und Visualisierung im KI-Zeitalter: Herausforderungen und Chancen
de.summary: Dieses Papier untersucht Herausforderungen in menschenzentrierten Datenanalysesystemen, die durch KI-Fortschritte eingeführt werden, einschließlich Latenz-Misalignment, Skalierbarkeitsbeschränkungen und Unsicherheiten durch Foundation Models. Es fordert eine Neudefinition von Mensch-Maschinen-Rollen und die Einbeziehung kognitiver und perceptueller Prinzipien in interaktive Datenanalysesysteme.

es.title: Interacción, Exploración y Visualización Humano-Datos en la Era de la IA: Desafíos y Oportunidades
es.summary: Este artículo investiga desafíos en sistemas de análisis de datos centrados en el ser humano introducidos por avances en IA, incluyendo desalineación de latencia, restricciones de escalabilidad e incertidumbre de modelos de fundación. Hace un llamado a redefinir los roles humano-máquina e incorporar principios cognitivos y perceptivos en sistemas de análisis de datos interactivos.

title: EigenData: A Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Platform for Function-Calling Data Synthesis, Auditing, and Repair
summary: EigenData is a multi-agent platform automating the complete lifecycle of function-calling agent training data through specialized subsystems for database, environment, and trajectory generation. The paper demonstrates effectiveness by auditing the Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard and introducing outcome-aware evaluation metrics for improved correlation with functional correctness.
content:
arXiv:2603.05553v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Function-calling agents -- large language models that invoke tools and APIs -- require high-quality, domain-specific training data spanning executable environments, backing databases, and diverse multi-turn trajectories. We introduce EigenData, an integrated, self-evolving platform that automates the full data lifecycle through a multi-agent architecture. A top-level orchestrator, EigenCore, coordinates three specialized sub-systems: DatabaseAgent for realistic domain database construction, CodingAgent for verified executable environment generation with iterative test-debug loops, and DataAgent for multi-turn trajectory synthesis with self-evolving prompt optimization. Cross-component feedback ensures consistency across all artifacts. We apply EigenData to audit and repair the Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard (BFCL-V3), identifying systematic errors in function schemas, implementations, and reference trajectories, automatically correcting them through coordinated schema refinement, code-level bug fixes, and trajectory modification, and introducing an outcome-aware evaluation protocol that assesses task success via database-state correctness rather than turn-level trajectory matching. We demonstrate that the repaired benchmark, coupled with outcome-aware metrics, produces model rankings substantially better correlated with human judgments of functional correctness.

zh.title: EigenData：用于函数调用数据合成、审计和修复的自进化多智能体平台
zh.summary: EigenData是一个多智能体平台，通过专门的数据库、环境和轨迹生成子系统，自动化函数调用智能体训练数据的完整生命周期。该论文通过审计伯克利函数调用排行榜并引入结果感知评估指标来展示有效性，以改进与功能正确性的关联。

fr.title: EigenData : Une Plateforme Multi-Agents Auto-Évolutive pour la Synthèse de Données d'Appel de Fonction, l'Audit et la Réparation
fr.summary: EigenData est une plateforme multi-agents automatisant le cycle de vie complet des données d'entraînement d'agents d'appel de fonction à travers des sous-systèmes spécialisés pour la génération de base de données, d'environnement et de trajectoire. L'article démontre son efficacité en auditant le Leaderboard des Appels de Fonction de Berkeley et en introduisant des métriques d'évaluation conscientes des résultats pour une meilleure corrélation avec la justesse fonctionnelle.

de.title: EigenData: Eine selbst-evolvierende Multi-Agent-Plattform für Funktionsaufrufdaten-Synthese, Auditing und Reparatur
de.summary: EigenData ist eine Multi-Agent-Plattform, die den gesamten Lebenszyklus von Funktionsaufruf-Agent-Trainingsdaten durch spezialisierte Subsysteme für Datenbank-, Umgebungs- und Trajektoriengenerierung automatisiert. Das Papier demonstriert die Effektivität durch Auditing des Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboards und die Einführung von outcome-aware Evaluierungsmetriken für verbesserte Korrelation mit funktionaler Korrektheit.

es.title: EigenData: Una Plataforma Multi-Agente Auto-Evolucionante para la Síntesis, Auditoría y Reparación de Datos de Llamadas de Función
es.summary: EigenData es una plataforma multi-agente que automatiza el ciclo de vida completo de datos de entrenamiento de agentes de llamadas de función a través de subsistemas especializados para generación de bases de datos, entorno y trayectoria. El artículo demuestra su efectividad auditando el Ranking de Llamadas de Función de Berkeley e introduciendo métricas de evaluación conscientes de resultados para una mejor correlación con la corrección funcional.

title: Towards Efficient and Stable Ocean State Forecasting: A Continuous-Time Koopman Approach
summary: This paper investigates Continuous-Time Koopman Autoencoders as lightweight surrogates for ocean state forecasting, achieving stable long-horizon predictions with bounded error growth. The approach preserves bulk energy spectra and statistical properties while providing orders-of-magnitude faster inference than numerical solvers for hybrid climate models.
content:
arXiv:2603.05560v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We investigate the Continuous-Time Koopman Autoencoder (CT-KAE) as a lightweight surrogate model for long-horizon ocean state forecasting in a two-layer quasi-geostrophic (QG) system. By projecting nonlinear dynamics into a latent space governed by a linear ordinary differential equation, the model enforces structured and interpretable temporal evolution while enabling temporally resolution-invariant forecasting via a matrix exponential formulation. Across 2083-day rollouts, CT-KAE exhibits bounded error growth and stable large-scale statistics, in contrast to autoregressive Transformer baselines which exhibit gradual error amplification and energy drift over long rollouts. While fine-scale turbulent structures are partially dissipated, bulk energy spectra, enstrophy evolution, and autocorrelation structure remain consistent over long horizons. The model achieves orders-of-magnitude faster inference compared to the numerical solver, suggesting that continuous-time Koopman surrogates offer a promising backbone for efficient and stable hybrid physical-machine learning climate models.

zh.title: 面向高效稳定的海洋状态预报：连续时间Koopman方法
zh.summary: 本文研究了连续时间Koopman自动编码器作为海洋状态预报的轻量级替代品，实现了误差增长有限的稳定长期预测。该方法保留了体积能量谱和统计特性，同时与混合气候模型的数值求解器相比提供了数个数量级更快的推理速度。

fr.title: Vers une Prévision d'État Océanique Efficace et Stable : Une Approche Koopman en Temps Continu
fr.summary: Cet article enquête sur les Autoencodeurs Koopman en Temps Continu comme substituts légers pour la prévision d'état océanique, réalisant des prédictions stables à long horizon avec une croissance d'erreur bornée. L'approche préserve les spectres d'énergie globaux et les propriétés statistiques tout en fournissant une inférence des ordres de magnitude plus rapide que les solveurs numériques pour les modèles climatiques hybrides.

de.title: Hin zu effizienter und stabiler Meereszustandsprognose: Ein kontinuierlich-zeitlicher Koopman-Ansatz
de.summary: Dieses Papier untersucht kontinuierlich-zeitliche Koopman-Autoencoder als leichte Surrogate für die Meereszustandsprognose und erreicht stabile Langzeitvorhersagen mit begrenztem Fehlerwachstum. Der Ansatz bewahrt die Gesamtenergiesspektren und statistischen Eigenschaften, während er eine um mehrere Größenordnungen schnellere Inferenz als numerische Löser für hybride Klimamodelle bietet.

es.title: Hacia una Predicción Eficiente y Estable del Estado Oceánico: Un Enfoque Koopman de Tiempo Continuo
es.summary: Este artículo investiga Autoencodificadores Koopman de Tiempo Continuo como sustitutos ligeros para la predicción del estado oceánico, logrando predicciones estables de largo horizonte con crecimiento de error acotado. El enfoque preserva los espectros de energía globales y las propiedades estadísticas mientras proporciona inferencia órdenes de magnitud más rápida que los solucionadores numéricos para modelos climáticos híbridos.

title: Model Change for Description Logic Concepts
summary: The paper introduces a formal framework for modifying description logic concepts in light of models, distinguishing between eviction (removal), reception (incorporation), and revision (combined operations). The authors prove that revision does not reduce to simple combinations of eviction and reception.
content:
arXiv:2603.05562v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We consider the problem of modifying a description logic concept in light of models represented as pointed interpretations. We call this setting model change, and distinguish three main kinds of changes: eviction, which consists of only removing models; reception, which incorporates models; and revision, which combines removal with incorporation of models in a single operation. We introduce a formal notion of revision and argue that it does not reduce to a simple combination of eviction and reception, contrary to intuition. We provide positive and negative results on the compatibility of eviction and reception for EL and ALC description logic concepts and on the compatibility of revision for ALC concepts.

zh.title: 描述逻辑概念的模型变化
zh.summary: 该论文为根据模型修改描述逻辑概念引入了一个形式化框架，区分了驱逐(移除)、接纳(纳入)和修订(综合操作)。作者证明修订不能简化为驱逐和接纳的简单组合。

fr.title: Changement de modèle pour les concepts de logique de description
fr.summary: L'article introduit un cadre formel pour modifier les concepts de logique de description à la lumière des modèles, en distinguant l'éviction (suppression), la réception (incorporation) et la révision (opérations combinées). Les auteurs prouvent que la révision ne se réduit pas à de simples combinaisons d'éviction et de réception.

de.title: Modelländerung für Beschreibungslogik-Konzepte
de.summary: Das Papier stellt einen formalen Rahmen zur Änderung von Beschreibungslogik-Konzepten angesichts von Modellen vor und unterscheidet zwischen Eviction (Entfernung), Rezeption (Inkorporation) und Revision (kombinierte Operationen). Die Autoren beweisen, dass Revision nicht auf einfache Kombinationen von Eviction und Rezeption reduzierbar ist.

es.title: Cambio de modelo para conceptos de lógica de descripción
es.summary: El artículo introduce un marco formal para modificar conceptos de lógica de descripción a la luz de modelos, distinguiendo entre evicción (eliminación), recepción (incorporación) y revisión (operaciones combinadas). Los autores demuestran que la revisión no se reduce a simples combinaciones de evicción y recepción.

title: When AI Levels the Playing Field: Skill Homogenization, Asset Concentration, and Two Regimes of Inequality
summary: The paper formalizes a paradox: generative AI may equalize individual performance while widening aggregate inequality by concentrating wealth in complementary assets. The analysis identifies two distinct economic regimes whose boundary depends on AI's technology structure and labor market institutions.
content:
arXiv:2603.05565v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Generative AI compresses within-task skill differences while shifting economic value toward concentrated complementary assets, creating an apparent paradox: the technology that equalizes individual performance may widen aggregate inequality. We formalize this tension in a task-based model with endogenous education, employer screening, and heterogeneous firms. The model yields two regimes whose boundary depends on AI's technology structure (proprietary vs.\ commodity) and labor market institutions (rent-sharing elasticity, asset concentration). A scenario analysis via Method of Simulated Moments, matching six empirical targets, disciplines the model's quantitative magnitudes; a sensitivity decomposition reveals that the five non-$\Delta$Gini moments identify mechanism rates but not the aggregate sign, which at the calibrated parameters is pinned by $m_6$ and $\xi$, while AI's technology structure ($\eta_1$ vs.\ $\eta_0$) independently crosses the boundary. The contribution is the mechanism -- not a verdict on the sign. Occupation-level regressions using BLS OEWS data (2019--2023) illustrate why such data cannot test the model's task-level predictions. The predictions are testable with within-occupation, within-task panel data that do not yet exist at scale.

zh.title: 当人工智能创造公平竞争环境：技能同质化、资产集中和不平等的两种制度
zh.summary: 该论文将一个悖论形式化：生成式人工智能可能会均衡个人表现，同时通过在互补资产中集中财富来扩大总体不平等。该分析确定了两个不同的经济制度，其边界取决于人工智能的技术结构和劳动力市场机构。

fr.title: Quand l'IA égalise les règles du jeu : homogénéisation des compétences, concentration des actifs et deux régimes d'inégalité
fr.summary: L'article formalise un paradoxe : l'IA générative peut égaliser les performances individuelles tout en élargissant les inégalités globales en concentrant la richesse dans les actifs complémentaires. L'analyse identifie deux régimes économiques distincts dont la limite dépend de la structure technologique de l'IA et des institutions du marché du travail.

de.title: Wenn KI das Spielfeld ausgleicht: Homogenisierung von Fähigkeiten, Vermögenskonzentration und zwei Ungleichheitsregime
de.summary: Das Papier formalisiert ein Paradoxon: Generative KI kann die individuelle Leistung angleichen, während sie die Gesamtungleichheit durch Vermögenskonzentration bei komplementären Vermögenswerten vergrößert. Die Analyse identifiziert zwei unterschiedliche Wirtschaftsregime, deren Grenze von der Technologiestruktur der KI und den Arbeitsmarktinstitutionen abhängt.

es.title: Cuando la IA iguala las reglas del juego: homogeneización de habilidades, concentración de activos y dos regímenes de desigualdad
es.summary: El artículo formaliza una paradoja: la IA generativa puede igualar el desempeño individual mientras amplía la desigualdad agregada concentrando la riqueza en activos complementarios. El análisis identifica dos regímenes económicos distintos cuyo límite depende de la estructura tecnológica de la IA y las instituciones del mercado laboral.

title: CBR-to-SQL: Rethinking Retrieval-based Text-to-SQL using Case-based Reasoning in the Healthcare Domain
summary: The paper proposes CBR-to-SQL, a case-based reasoning framework that improves large language models' ability to generate SQL queries from natural language in healthcare settings, demonstrating superior robustness compared to standard retrieval-augmented generation approaches. The method addresses challenges specific to medical terminology and domain variability.
content:
arXiv:2603.05569v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Extracting insights from Electronic Health Record (EHR) databases often requires SQL expertise, creating a barrier for healthcare decision-making and research. While a promising approach is to use Large Language Models (LLMs) to translate natural language questions to SQL via Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), adapting this approach to the medical domain is non-trivial. Standard RAG relies on single-step retrieval from a static pool of examples, which struggles with the variability and noise of medical terminology and jargon. This often leads to anti-patterns such as expanding the task demonstration pool to improve coverage, which in turn introduces noise and scalability problems. To address this, we introduce CBR-to-SQL, a framework inspired by Case-Based Reasoning (CBR). It represents question-SQL pairs as reusable, abstract case templates and utilizes a two-stage retrieval process that first captures logical structure and then resolves relevant entities. Evaluated on MIMICSQL, CBR-to-SQL achieves state-of-the-art logical form accuracy and competitive execution accuracy. More importantly, it demonstrates higher sample efficiency and robustness than standard RAG approaches, particularly under data scarcity and retrieval perturbations.

zh.title: CBR-to-SQL：在医疗保健领域使用案例推理重新思考基于检索的文本到SQL
zh.summary: 该论文提出了CBR-to-SQL，一个基于案例推理的框架，改进了大型语言模型在医疗保健设置中从自然语言生成SQL查询的能力，相比标准的检索增强生成方法，表现出更优越的鲁棒性。该方法解决了特定于医学术语和领域变异性的挑战。

fr.title: CBR-to-SQL : Repenser le texte vers SQL basé sur la récupération à l'aide du raisonnement basé sur des cas dans le domaine de la santé
fr.summary: L'article propose CBR-to-SQL, un cadre de raisonnement basé sur des cas qui améliore la capacité des grands modèles de langage à générer des requêtes SQL à partir du langage naturel dans les environnements de santé, démontrant une robustesse supérieure par rapport aux approches standard de génération augmentée par la récupération. La méthode aborde les défis spécifiques à la terminologie médicale et à la variabilité du domaine.

de.title: CBR-to-SQL: Überdenken von abrufgestütztem Text-zu-SQL mit fallbasiertem Denken im Gesundheitswesen
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt CBR-to-SQL vor, ein auf Fällen basierendes Reasoning-Framework, das die Fähigkeit großer Sprachmodelle verbessert, SQL-Abfragen aus natürlicher Sprache in Gesundheitseinrichtungen zu generieren, und eine überlegene Robustheit gegenüber Standard-Retrieval-augmented-Generation-Ansätzen demonstriert. Die Methode befasst sich mit Herausforderungen, die spezifisch für medizinische Terminologie und Domänenvariabilität sind.

es.title: CBR-to-SQL: Repensar el texto a SQL basado en recuperación usando razonamiento basado en casos en el dominio de la salud
es.summary: El artículo propone CBR-to-SQL, un marco de razonamiento basado en casos que mejora la capacidad de los grandes modelos de lenguaje para generar consultas SQL a partir del lenguaje natural en entornos de salud, demostrando una robustez superior en comparación con los enfoques estándar de generación aumentada por recuperación. El método aborda desafíos específicos para la terminología médica y la variabilidad del dominio.

title: PRISM: Personalized Refinement of Imitation Skills for Manipulation via Human Instructions
summary: PRISM presents an instruction-conditioned method for refining imitation learning policies in robotic manipulation through reinforcement learning guided by human feedback. The approach enables policy adaptation to new configurations and constraints while improving robustness and data efficiency.
content:
arXiv:2603.05574v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This paper presents PRISM: an instruction-conditioned refinement method for imitation policies in robotic manipulation. This approach bridges Imitation Learning (IL) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) frameworks into a seamless pipeline, such that an imitation policy on a broad generic task, generated from a set of user-guided demonstrations, can be refined through reinforcement to generate new unseen fine-grain behaviours. The refinement process follows the Eureka paradigm, where reward functions for RL are iteratively generated from an initial natural-language task description. Presented approach, builds on top of this mechanism to adapt a refined IL policy of a generic task to new goal configurations and the introduction of constraints by adding also human feedback correction on intermediate rollouts, enabling policy reusability and therefore data efficiency. Results for a pick-and-place task in a simulated scenario show that proposed method outperforms policies without human feedback, improving robustness on deployment and reducing computational burden.

zh.title: PRISM：通过人类指导的操纵模仿技能个性化精炼
zh.summary: PRISM提出了一种指令条件化方法，通过人类反馈指导的强化学习来精炼机器人操纵中的模仿学习策略。该方法使策略能够适应新的配置和约束，同时提高鲁棒性和数据效率。

fr.title: PRISM : Raffinement Personnalisé des Compétences d'Imitation pour la Manipulation via les Instructions Humaines
fr.summary: PRISM présente une méthode conditionnée par des instructions pour affiner les politiques d'apprentissage par imitation en manipulation robotique par le biais d'un apprentissage par renforcement guidé par les retours humains. L'approche permet l'adaptation des politiques à de nouvelles configurations et contraintes tout en améliorant la robustesse et l'efficacité des données.

de.title: PRISM: Personalisierte Verbesserung von Imitationsfähigkeiten für die Manipulation durch menschliche Anweisungen
de.summary: PRISM präsentiert eine instruktionsgesteuerte Methode zur Verfeinerung von Imitationslernrichtlinien in der Robotermanipulation durch verstärktes Lernen, das durch menschliches Feedback geleitet wird. Der Ansatz ermöglicht eine Anpassung der Richtlinien an neue Konfigurationen und Einschränkungen und verbessert gleichzeitig die Robustheit und Dateneffizienz.

es.title: PRISM: Refinamiento Personalizado de Habilidades de Imitación para la Manipulación mediante Instrucciones Humanas
es.summary: PRISM presenta un método condicionado por instrucciones para refinar las políticas de aprendizaje por imitación en la manipulación robótica mediante el aprendizaje por refuerzo guiado por retroalimentación humana. El enfoque permite la adaptación de políticas a nuevas configuraciones y restricciones mientras mejora la robustez y la eficiencia de datos.

title: Tool-Genesis: A Task-Driven Tool Creation Benchmark for Self-Evolving Language Agent
summary: Tool-Genesis introduces a diagnostic benchmark for evaluating whether language agents can construct task-relevant tools solely from abstract requirements without preset specifications. The research reveals that even state-of-the-art models struggle with precise interface design and executable logic generation.
content:
arXiv:2603.05578v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Research on self-evolving language agents has accelerated, drawing increasing attention to their ability to create, adapt, and maintain tools from task requirements. However, existing benchmarks predominantly rely on predefined specifications, which limits scalability and hinders truly autonomous evolution. While recent studies attempt to dynamically generate tools, they primarily emphasize downstream performance, resulting in a "black-box" evaluation that makes it difficult to attribute failures to specific causes. To address this, we propose Tool-Genesis, a diagnostic benchmark designed to quantify agent capabilities across multiple dimensions, including interface compliance, functional correctness, and downstream utility. Tool-Genesis evaluates whether agents can construct task-relevant tools solely from abstract requirements (without preset specifications) and use them to solve realistic problems. Crucially, we find that even state-of-the-art models struggle to produce precise tool interfaces or executable logic in a one-shot setting. These minor initial flaws are amplified through the pipeline, leading to a sharp degradation in downstream metrics. We hope Tool-Genesis will guide future research toward training and steering models to synthesize persistent, general-purpose tools that better address real-world challenges.

zh.title: Tool-Genesis：自进化语言代理的任务驱动工具创建基准
zh.summary: Tool-Genesis引入了一个诊断基准来评估语言代理是否能够仅从抽象需求构建任务相关工具，而无需预设规范。该研究表明，即使是最先进的模型在精确的接口设计和可执行逻辑生成方面也存在困难。

fr.title: Tool-Genesis : Un Benchmark de Création d'Outils Piloté par les Tâches pour un Agent Linguistique Auto-Évoluant
fr.summary: Tool-Genesis introduit un benchmark de diagnostic pour évaluer si les agents linguistiques peuvent construire des outils pertinents à la tâche à partir de seules exigences abstraites sans spécifications prédéfinies. La recherche révèle que même les modèles de pointe ont du mal à concevoir des interfaces précises et à générer une logique exécutable.

de.title: Tool-Genesis: Ein aufgabengestütztes Tool-Erstellungs-Benchmark für selbst-evolvierenden Sprachagenten
de.summary: Tool-Genesis führt einen Diagnose-Benchmark ein, um zu bewerten, ob Sprachagenten aufgabenrelevante Tools nur aus abstrakten Anforderungen ohne vorgegebene Spezifikationen konstruieren können. Die Forschung zeigt, dass selbst modernste Modelle bei präzisem Interface-Design und der Generierung ausführbarer Logik Schwierigkeiten haben.

es.title: Tool-Genesis: Un Benchmark de Creación de Herramientas Impulsado por Tareas para Agentes de Lenguaje Autoevolucionales
es.summary: Tool-Genesis introduce un benchmark de diagnóstico para evaluar si los agentes de lenguaje pueden construir herramientas relevantes para tareas únicamente a partir de requisitos abstractos sin especificaciones predefinidas. La investigación revela que incluso los modelos más avanzados luchan con el diseño preciso de interfaces y la generación de lógica ejecutable.

title: Spatiotemporal Heterogeneity of AI-Driven Traffic Flow Patterns and Land Use Interaction: A GeoAI-Based Analysis of Multimodal Urban Mobility
summary: The paper proposes a GeoAI framework integrating machine learning methods to model spatiotemporal traffic flow patterns and their interactions with urban land use across multiple transportation modes. The framework significantly outperforms existing approaches and identifies land use configuration as a key predictor of mobility demand.
content:
arXiv:2603.05581v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Urban traffic flow is governed by the complex, nonlinear interaction between land use configuration and spatiotemporally heterogeneous mobility demand. Conventional global regression and time-series models cannot simultaneously capture these multi-scale dynamics across multiple travel modes. This study proposes a GeoAI Hybrid analytical framework that sequentially integrates Multiscale Geographically Weighted Regression (MGWR), Random Forest (RF), and Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks (ST-GCN) to model the spatiotemporal heterogeneity of traffic flow patterns and their interaction with land use across three mobility modes: motor vehicle, public transit, and active transport. Applying the framework to an empirically calibrated dataset of 350 traffic analysis zones across six cities spanning two contrasting urban morphologies, four key findings emerge: (i) the GeoAI Hybrid achieves a root mean squared error (RMSE) of 0.119 and an R^2 of 0.891, outperforming all benchmarks by 23-62%; (ii) SHAP analysis identifies land use mix as the strongest predictor for motor vehicle flows and transit stop density as the strongest predictor for public transit; (iii) DBSCAN clustering identifies five functionally distinct urban traffic typologies with a silhouette score of 0.71, and GeoAI Hybrid residuals exhibit Moran's I=0.218 (p=0.78) and limited cross-cluster generalisability, underscoring the primacy of urban morphological context. The framework offers planners and transportation engineers an interpretable, scalable toolkit for evidence-based multimodal mobility management and land use policy design.

zh.title: AI驱动的交通流模式和土地利用相互作用的时空异质性：基于地理人工智能的多模式城市移动性分析
zh.summary: 该论文提出了一个地理人工智能框架，集成机器学习方法来模拟时空交通流模式及其与多种交通方式的城市土地利用的相互作用。该框架明显优于现有方法，并将土地利用配置确定为流动需求的关键预测因子。

fr.title: Hétérogénéité Spatiotemporelle des Modèles de Flux de Trafic Pilotés par l'IA et Interaction de l'Utilisation des Terres : Une Analyse Basée sur la GéoIA de la Mobilité Urbaine Multimodale
fr.summary: L'article propose un cadre GéoIA intégrant des méthodes d'apprentissage automatique pour modéliser les modèles de flux de trafic spatiotemporels et leurs interactions avec l'utilisation des terres urbaines dans plusieurs modes de transport. Le cadre surpasse considérablement les approches existantes et identifie la configuration de l'utilisation des terres comme un prédicteur clé de la demande de mobilité.

de.title: Räumliche Heterogenität von KI-gesteuerten Verkehrsflussmustern und Landnutzungsinteraktion: Eine GeoAI-basierte Analyse der multimodalen urbanen Mobilität
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt ein GeoAI-Framework vor, das Maschinenlernmethoden integriert, um räumlich-zeitliche Verkehrsfluss-Muster und deren Wechselwirkungen mit der städtischen Landnutzung über mehrere Verkehrsmodi hinweg zu modellieren. Das Framework übertrifft bestehende Ansätze erheblich und identifiziert die Landnutzungskonfiguration als einen Schlüsselprädikator der Mobilitätsnachfrage.

es.title: Heterogeneidad Espacio-Temporal de Patrones de Flujo de Tráfico Impulsados por IA e Interacción de Uso de Tierra: Un Análisis Basado en GeoIA de la Movilidad Urbana Multimodal
es.summary: El artículo propone un marco GeoIA que integra métodos de aprendizaje automático para modelar patrones de flujo de tráfico espacio-temporales y sus interacciones con el uso de tierras urbanas en múltiples modos de transporte. El marco supera significativamente los enfoques existentes e identifica la configuración de uso de tierras como un predictor clave de la demanda de movilidad.

title: On the Value of Tokeniser Pretraining in Physics Foundation Models
summary: The study investigates tokenizer pretraining for physics foundation models, demonstrating that domain-aligned pretraining reduces computational costs and improves accuracy for downstream tasks. The research provides practical guidance for efficiently training physics emulators while highlighting the importance of strategic data selection.
content:
arXiv:2603.05598v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We investigate the impact of tokeniser pretraining on the accuracy and efficiency of physics emulation. Modern high-resolution simulations produce vast volumes of data spanning diverse physical regimes and scales. Training foundation models to learn the dynamics underlying such data enables the modelling of complex multiphysics phenomena, especially in data-limited settings. The emerging class of physics foundation models typically aims to learn two tasks jointly: (i) extracting compact representations of high-resolution spatiotemporal data, and (ii) capturing governing physical dynamics. However, learning both tasks from scratch simultaneously can impede the effectiveness of either process. We demonstrate that pretraining the tokeniser with an autoencoding objective prior to training the dynamics model enhances computational efficiency for downstream tasks. Notably, the magnitude of this benefit depends on domain alignment: pretraining on the same physical system as the downstream task yields the largest improvements, while pretraining on other systems provides moderate gains. In-domain pretraining reduces VRMSE by 64% after 10,500 training steps compared to training from scratch. To our knowledge, this is the first systematic investigation of tokeniser pretraining for physics foundation models. We further introduce flexible spatiotemporal compression operations that extend causal convolutions to support runtime-adjustable compression ratios, enabling efficient adaptation to diverse downstream tasks. Our findings provide practical guidance for training efficient physics emulators and highlight the importance of strategic pretraining data selection.

zh.title: 关于分词器预训练在物理基础模型中的价值
zh.summary: 该研究调查了物理基础模型的分词器预训练，证明了领域对齐的预训练可以降低计算成本并提高下游任务的准确性。该研究为高效训练物理模拟器提供了实用指导，同时强调了战略性数据选择的重要性。

fr.title: Sur la valeur de la pré-entraînement du tokeniseur dans les modèles fondamentaux de physique
fr.summary: L'étude enquête sur la pré-entraînement du tokeniseur pour les modèles fondamentaux de physique, démontrant que la pré-entraînement alignée au domaine réduit les coûts informatiques et améliore la précision pour les tâches en aval. La recherche fournit des conseils pratiques pour l'entraînement efficace des émulateurs de physique tout en soulignant l'importance de la sélection stratégique des données.

de.title: Über den Wert der Tokenizer-Vorschulung in physikalischen Grundmodellen
de.summary: Die Studie untersucht die Tokenizer-Vorschulung für physikalische Grundmodelle und zeigt, dass domänengerichtete Vorschulung die Rechenkosten senkt und die Genauigkeit für nachgelagerte Aufgaben verbessert. Die Forschung bietet praktische Anleitung für das effiziente Training von physikalischen Emulatoren und unterstreicht die Bedeutung einer strategischen Datenauswahl.

es.title: Sobre el valor del preentrenamiento del tokenizador en modelos fundamentales de física
es.summary: El estudio investiga el preentrenamiento del tokenizador para modelos fundamentales de física, demostrando que el preentrenamiento alineado con el dominio reduce los costos computacionales y mejora la precisión para tareas posteriores. La investigación proporciona orientación práctica para entrenar eficientemente emuladores de física mientras destaca la importancia de la selección estratégica de datos.

title: DreamCAD: Scaling Multi-modal CAD Generation using Differentiable Parametric Surfaces
summary: DreamCAD presents a multi-modal generative framework for producing editable CAD models from text, images, and point clouds using differentiable parametric surfaces. The paper introduces CADCap-1M, the largest CAD captioning dataset, and achieves state-of-the-art results across multiple modalities.
content:
arXiv:2603.05607v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Computer-Aided Design (CAD) relies on structured and editable geometric representations, yet existing generative methods are constrained by small annotated datasets with explicit design histories or boundary representation (BRep) labels. Meanwhile, millions of unannotated 3D meshes remain untapped, limiting progress in scalable CAD generation. To address this, we propose DreamCAD, a multi-modal generative framework that directly produces editable BReps from point-level supervision, without CAD-specific annotations. DreamCAD represents each BRep as a set of parametric patches (e.g., B\'ezier surfaces) and uses a differentiable tessellation method to generate meshes. This enables large-scale training on 3D datasets while reconstructing connected and editable surfaces. Furthermore, we introduce CADCap-1M, the largest CAD captioning dataset to date, with 1M+ descriptions generated using GPT-5 for advancing text-to-CAD research. DreamCAD achieves state-of-the-art performance on ABC and Objaverse benchmarks across text, image, and point modalities, improving geometric fidelity and surpassing 75% user preference. Code and dataset will be publicly available.

zh.title: DreamCAD：使用可微分参数化表面的多模式CAD生成缩放
zh.summary: DreamCAD提出了一个多模式生成框架，用于从文本、图像和点云生成可编辑的CAD模型，使用可微分参数化表面。该论文引入了CADCap-1M，最大的CAD标题数据集，并在多个模态中实现了最先进的结果。

fr.title: DreamCAD : Mise à l'échelle de la génération CAO multi-modale à l'aide de surfaces paramétriques différentiables
fr.summary: DreamCAD présente un framework génératif multi-modal pour produire des modèles CAO modifiables à partir de texte, d'images et de nuages de points en utilisant des surfaces paramétriques différentiables. L'article introduit CADCap-1M, le plus grand ensemble de données de sous-titrage CAO, et atteint des résultats de pointe sur plusieurs modalités.

de.title: DreamCAD: Skalierung der multimodalen CAD-Generierung mit differenzierbaren parametrischen Oberflächen
de.summary: DreamCAD präsentiert ein multimodales generatives Framework zur Erzeugung bearbeitbarer CAD-Modelle aus Text, Bildern und Punktwolken mit differenzierbaren parametrischen Oberflächen. Das Papier führt CADCap-1M, den größten CAD-Beschriftungsdatensatz, ein und erreicht modernste Ergebnisse über mehrere Modalitäten hinweg.

es.title: DreamCAD: Escalado de generación CAD multimodal usando superficies paramétricas diferenciables
es.summary: DreamCAD presenta un marco generativo multimodal para producir modelos CAD editables a partir de texto, imágenes y nubes de puntos utilizando superficies paramétricas diferenciables. El artículo introduce CADCap-1M, el conjunto de datos de subtítulos CAD más grande, y logra resultados de última generación en múltiples modalidades.

title: RACAS: Controlling Diverse Robots With a Single Agentic System
summary: RACAS introduces a cooperative agentic architecture using language models to control diverse robotic platforms without retraining, requiring only natural language descriptions of the robot and task specifications. The system demonstrates consistent performance across wheeled robots, multi-jointed limbs, and underwater vehicles.
content:
arXiv:2603.05621v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Many robotic platforms expose an API through which external software can command their actuators and read their sensors. However, transitioning from these low-level interfaces to high-level autonomous behaviour requires a complicated pipeline, whose components demand distinct areas of expertise. Existing approaches to bridging this gap either require retraining for every new embodiment or have only been validated across structurally similar platforms. We introduce RACAS (Robot-Agnostic Control via Agentic Systems), a cooperative agentic architecture in which three LLM/VLM-based modules (Monitors, a Controller, and a Memory Curator) communicate exclusively through natural language to provide closed-loop robot control. RACAS requires only a natural language description of the robot, a definition of available actions, and a task specification; no source code, model weights, or reward functions need to be modified to move between platforms. We evaluate RACAS on several tasks using a wheeled ground robot, a recently published novel multi-jointed robotic limb, and an underwater vehicle. RACAS consistently solved all assigned tasks across these radically different platforms, demonstrating the potential of agentic AI to substantially reduce the barrier to prototyping robotic solutions.

zh.title: RACAS：用单一代理系统控制多种机器人
zh.summary: RACAS引入了一个协作代理架构，使用语言模型来控制不同的机器人平台，无需重新训练，仅需要机器人和任务规范的自然语言描述。该系统在轮式机器人、多关节肢体和水下车辆中表现出一致的性能。

fr.title: RACAS : Contrôler des robots diversifiés avec un système agentique unique
fr.summary: RACAS introduit une architecture agentique coopérative utilisant des modèles de langage pour contrôler diverses plates-formes robotiques sans réentraînement, ne nécessitant que des descriptions en langage naturel du robot et des spécifications des tâches. Le système démontre des performances cohérentes sur les robots à roues, les membres multi-articulés et les véhicules sous-marins.

de.title: RACAS: Steuerung diverser Roboter mit einem einzigen agentengesteuerten System
de.summary: RACAS führt eine kooperative Agenten-Architektur ein, die Sprachmodelle verwendet, um verschiedene Roboterplattformen ohne Umschulung zu steuern und erfordert nur natürlichsprachliche Beschreibungen des Roboters und Aufgabenspezifikationen. Das System zeigt konsistente Leistung über Radroboter, mehrgelenkige Gliedmaßen und Unterwasserfahrzeuge hinweg.

es.title: RACAS: Controlar robots diversos con un único sistema agencial
es.summary: RACAS introduce una arquitectura agencial cooperativa que utiliza modelos de lenguaje para controlar diversas plataformas robóticas sin reentrenamiento, requiriendo solo descripciones en lenguaje natural del robot y especificaciones de tareas. El sistema demuestra un rendimiento consistente en robots con ruedas, extremidades multiarticuladas y vehículos submarinos.

title: Adversarial Batch Representation Augmentation for Batch Correction in High-Content Cellular Screening
summary: The paper proposes Adversarial Batch Representation Augmentation (ABRA) to correct technical variations in high-content cellular screening by framing batch correction as a domain generalization problem. ABRA achieves state-of-the-art results on large-scale benchmarks while preserving fine-grained classification accuracy.
content:
arXiv:2603.05622v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: High-Content Screening routinely generates massive volumes of cell painting images for phenotypic profiling. However, technical variations across experimental executions inevitably induce biological batch (bio-batch) effects. These cause covariate shifts and degrade the generalization of deep learning models on unseen data. Existing batch correction methods typically rely on additional prior knowledge (e.g., treatment or cell culture information) or struggle to generalize to unseen bio-batches. In this work, we frame bio-batch mitigation as a Domain Generalization (DG) problem and propose Adversarial Batch Representation Augmentation (ABRA). ABRA explicitly models batch-wise statistical fluctuations by parameterizing feature statistics as structured uncertainties. Through a min-max optimization framework, it actively synthesizes worst-case bio-batch perturbations in the representation space, guided by a strict angular geometric margin to preserve fine-grained class discriminability. To prevent representation collapse during this adversarial exploration, we introduce a synergistic distribution alignment objective. Extensive evaluations on the large-scale RxRx1 and RxRx1-WILDS benchmarks demonstrate that ABRA establishes a new state-of-the-art for siRNA perturbation classification.

zh.title: 用于高内容细胞筛选批次校正的对抗批次表示增强
zh.summary: 该论文提出对抗批次表示增强(ABRA)，通过将批次校正框架化为域泛化问题来纠正高内容细胞筛选中的技术变异。ABRA在大规模基准上达到最先进的结果，同时保持了细粒度分类的准确性。

fr.title: Augmentation Adversariale de la Représentation par Lot pour la Correction par Lot dans le Dépistage Cellulaire à Haut Contenu
fr.summary: L'article propose l'Augmentation Adversariale de la Représentation par Lot (ABRA) pour corriger les variations techniques dans le dépistage cellulaire à haut contenu en formulant la correction par lot comme un problème de généralisation de domaine. ABRA obtient des résultats de pointe sur les grands benchmarks tout en préservant la précision de la classification fine.

de.title: Adversariale Batch-Darstellungs-Augmentation zur Batch-Korrektur im hochinhaltsreichen Zell-Screening
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt Adversariale Batch-Darstellungs-Augmentation (ABRA) vor, um technische Variationen beim hochinhaltsreichen Zell-Screening durch Formulierung der Batch-Korrektur als Domänengeneralisierungsproblem zu korrigieren. ABRA erreicht hochwertige Ergebnisse auf großen Benchmarks und bewahrt gleichzeitig die Genauigkeit der feinkornigen Klassifizierung.

es.title: Aumento Adversarial de Representación de Lotes para Corrección de Lotes en Cribado Celular de Alto Contenido
es.summary: El documento propone Aumento Adversarial de Representación de Lotes (ABRA) para corregir variaciones técnicas en el cribado celular de alto contenido al formular la corrección de lotes como un problema de generalización de dominio. ABRA logra resultados de última generación en puntos de referencia a gran escala mientras preserva la precisión de la clasificación de grano fino.

title: Post Fusion Bird's Eye View Feature Stabilization for Robust Multimodal 3D Detection
summary: The paper introduces a lightweight post-fusion module that stabilizes bird's-eye view features in camera-lidar fusion detectors, improving robustness under domain shift and sensor failures. The approach achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple corruption types while maintaining a minimal computational footprint.
content:
arXiv:2603.05623v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Camera-LiDAR fusion is widely used in autonomous driving to enable accurate 3D object detection. However, bird's-eye view (BEV) fusion detectors can degrade significantly under domain shift and sensor failures, limiting reliability in real-world deployment. Existing robustness approaches often require modifying the fusion architecture or retraining specialized models, making them difficult to integrate into already deployed systems.
  We propose a Post Fusion Stabilizer (PFS), a lightweight module that operates on intermediate BEV representations of existing detectors and produces a refined feature map for the original detection head. The design stabilizes feature statistics under domain shift, suppresses spatial regions affected by sensor degradation, and adaptively restores weakened cues through residual correction. Designed as a near-identity transformation, PFS preserves performance while improving robustness under diverse camera and LiDAR corruptions. Evaluations on the nuScenes benchmark demonstrate that PFS achieves state-of-the-art results in several failure modes, notably improving camera dropout robustness by +1.2% and low-light performance by +4.4% mAP while maintaining a lightweight footprint of only 3.3 M parameters.

zh.title: 用于稳健多模态3D检测的融后鸟瞰图特征稳定化
zh.summary: 该论文介绍了一个轻量级融后模块，可以稳定化摄像头-激光雷达融合检测器中的鸟瞰图特征，提高了在域转移和传感器故障下的鲁棒性。该方法在多种破坏类型上取得了最先进的结果，同时保持了最小的计算成本。

fr.title: Stabilisation des Caractéristiques de Vue Aérienne Après Fusion pour une Détection 3D Multimodale Robuste
fr.summary: L'article présente un module post-fusion léger qui stabilise les caractéristiques de vue aérienne dans les détecteurs de fusion caméra-lidar, améliorant la robustesse sous décalage de domaine et défaillances des capteurs. L'approche obtient des résultats de pointe sur plusieurs types de corruption tout en conservant une empreinte de calcul minimale.

de.title: Stabilisierung von Vogelperspektiv-Merkmalen nach der Fusion für robuste multimodale 3D-Erkennung
de.summary: Das Papier führt ein leichtes Post-Fusion-Modul ein, das Vogelperspektiv-Merkmale in Kamera-Lidar-Fusions-Detektoren stabilisiert und die Robustheit bei Domänenversatz und Sensorfehler verbessert.
