Der Ansatz erreicht hochwertige Ergebnisse bei mehreren Korruptionstypen und bewahrt gleichzeitig einen minimalen Rechenaufwand.

es.title: Estabilización de Características de Vista de Pájaro Después de la Fusión para Detección 3D Multimodal Robusta
es.summary: El documento introduce un módulo post-fusión ligero que estabiliza características de vista de pájaro en detectores de fusión cámara-lidar, mejorando la robustez bajo cambio de dominio y fallas de sensores. El enfoque logra resultados de última generación en múltiples tipos de corrupción mientras mantiene una huella computacional mínima.

title: Relational Semantic Reasoning on 3D Scene Graphs for Open World Interactive Object Search
summary: SCOUT enables robots to search for objects in household environments by reasoning about semantic relationships in 3D scene graphs, using lightweight models distilled from large language models. The method achieves computational efficiency comparable to embedding-based approaches while matching large language model performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.05642v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Open-world interactive object search in household environments requires understanding semantic relationships between objects and their surrounding context to guide exploration efficiently. Prior methods either rely on vision-language embeddings similarity, which does not reliably capture task-relevant relational semantics, or large language models (LLMs), which are too slow and costly for real-time deployment. We introduce SCOUT: Scene Graph-Based Exploration with Learned Utility for Open-World Interactive Object Search, a novel method that searches directly over 3D scene graphs by assigning utility scores to rooms, frontiers, and objects using relational exploration heuristics such as room-object containment and object-object co-occurrence. To make this practical without sacrificing open-vocabulary generalization, we propose an offline procedural distillation framework that extracts structured relational knowledge from LLMs into lightweight models for on-robot inference. Furthermore, we present SymSearch, a scalable symbolic benchmark for evaluating semantic reasoning in interactive object search tasks. Extensive evaluations across symbolic and simulation environments show that SCOUT outperforms embedding similarity-based methods and matches LLM-level performance while remaining computationally efficient. Finally, real-world experiments demonstrate effective transfer to physical environments, enabling open-world interactive object search under realistic sensing and navigation constraints.

zh.title: 用于开放世界交互式对象搜索的3D场景图关系语义推理
zh.summary: SCOUT通过推理3D场景图中的语义关系，使机器人能够在家庭环境中搜索对象，使用从大型语言模型中提取的轻量级模型。该方法在计算效率上与基于嵌入的方法相当，同时与大型语言模型的性能相匹配。

fr.title: Raisonnement Sémantique Relationnel sur les Graphes de Scènes 3D pour la Recherche d'Objets Interactifs en Monde Ouvert
fr.summary: SCOUT permet aux robots de rechercher des objets dans des environnements domestiques en raisonnant sur les relations sémantiques dans les graphes de scènes 3D, en utilisant des modèles légers distillés à partir de grands modèles de langage. La méthode atteint une efficacité informatique comparable aux approches basées sur les plongements tout en correspondant aux performances des grands modèles de langage.

de.title: Relationales semantisches Reasoning auf 3D-Szenengraphen für die offene interaktive Objektsuche
de.summary: SCOUT ermöglicht es Robotern, Objekte in Haushaltsumgebungen zu suchen, indem sie semantische Beziehungen in 3D-Szenengraphen analysieren und leichte Modelle verwenden, die aus großen Sprachmodellen destilliert wurden. Die Methode erreicht Recheneffizienz, die mit einbettungsgestützten Ansätzen vergleichbar ist, und entspricht gleichzeitig der Leistung großer Sprachmodelle.

es.title: Razonamiento Semántico Relacional en Gráficos de Escenas 3D para Búsqueda de Objetos Interactivos en Mundo Abierto
es.summary: SCOUT permite a los robots buscar objetos en entornos domésticos razonando sobre relaciones semánticas en gráficos de escenas 3D, utilizando modelos ligeros destilados de grandes modelos de lenguaje. El método logra eficiencia computacional comparable a los enfoques basados en incrustaciones mientras se adapta al rendimiento de los grandes modelos de lenguaje.

title: The Fragility Of Moral Judgment In Large Language Models
summary: Study of 2,939 Reddit moral dilemmas reveals LLM moral judgments are unstable to narrative changes, persuasion cues, and evaluation protocols. This instability raises reproducibility concerns when AI moral guidance depends on presentation rather than substance.
content:
arXiv:2603.05651v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: People increasingly use large language models (LLMs) for everyday moral and interpersonal guidance, yet these systems cannot interrogate missing context and judge dilemmas as presented. We introduce a perturbation framework for testing the stability and manipulability of LLM moral judgments while holding the underlying moral conflict constant. Using 2,939 dilemmas from r/AmItheAsshole (January-March 2025), we generate three families of content perturbations: surface edits (lexical/structural noise), point-of-view shifts (voice and stance neutralization), and persuasion cues (self-positioning, social proof, pattern admissions, victim framing). We also vary the evaluation protocol (output ordering, instruction placement, and unstructured prompting). We evaluated all variants with four models (GPT-4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet, DeepSeek V3, Qwen2.5-72B) (N=129,156 judgments).
  Surface perturbations produce low flip rates (7.5%), largely within the self-consistency noise floor (4-13%), whereas point-of-view shifts induce substantially higher instability (24.3%). A large subset of dilemmas (37.9%) is robust to surface noise yet flips under perspective changes, indicating that models condition on narrative voice as a pragmatic cue. Instability concentrates in morally ambiguous cases; scenarios where no party is assigned blame are most susceptible. Persuasion perturbations yield systematic directional shifts. Protocol choices dominate all other factors: agreement between structured protocols is only 67.6% (kappa=0.55), and only 35.7% of model-scenario units match across all three protocols. These results show that LLM moral judgments are co-produced by narrative form and task scaffolding, raising reproducibility and equity concerns when outcomes depend on presentation skill rather than moral substance.

zh.title: 大语言模型中道德判断的脆弱性
zh.summary: 对2,939条Reddit道德困境的研究表明，大语言模型的道德判断对叙述变化、劝说线索和评估协议不稳定。这种不稳定性在AI道德指导依赖于呈现而非实质时引发了可重复性问题。

fr.title: La Fragilité du Jugement Moral dans les Grands Modèles de Langage
fr.summary: L'étude de 2 939 dilemmes moraux Reddit révèle que les jugements moraux des LLM sont instables face aux changements narratifs, aux signaux de persuasion et aux protocoles d'évaluation. Cette instabilité soulève des préoccupations en matière de reproductibilité lorsque les conseils moraux de l'IA dépendent de la présentation plutôt que du contenu.

de.title: Die Zerbrechlichkeit moralischer Urteile in großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Eine Studie von 2.939 moralischen Dilemmas auf Reddit zeigt, dass moralische Urteile von LLMs gegenüber narrativen Veränderungen, Überzeugungssignalen und Bewertungsprotokollen instabil sind. Diese Instabilität wirft Fragen zur Reproduzierbarkeit auf, wenn KI-Moralführung von der Präsentation statt vom Inhalt abhängt.

es.title: La Fragilidad del Juicio Moral en Modelos de Lenguaje Grande
es.summary: Un estudio de 2.939 dilemas morales de Reddit revela que los juicios morales de LLM son inestables ante cambios narrativos, señales de persuasión y protocolos de evaluación. Esta inestabilidad plantea preocupaciones de reproducibilidad cuando la orientación moral de la IA depende de la presentación en lugar del contenido.

title: The DSA's Blind Spot: Algorithmic Audit of Advertising and Minor Profiling on TikTok
summary: EU regulations prohibit profiled advertising to minors but exclude influencer marketing. An algorithmic audit reveals TikTok still profiles minors through undisclosed promotional content, exposing a critical regulatory gap.
content:
arXiv:2603.05653v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Adolescents spend an increasing amount of their time in digital environments where their still-developing cognitive capacities leave them unable to recognize or resist commercial persuasion. Article 28(2) of the Digital Service Act (DSA) responds to this vulnerability by prohibiting profiling-based advertising to minors. However, the regulation's narrow definition of "advertisement" excludes current advertising practices including influencer marketing and promotional content that serve functionally equivalent commercial purposes. We provide the first empirical evidence of how this definitional gap operates in practice through an algorithmic audit of TikTok. Our approach deploys sock-puppet accounts simulating a pair of minor and adult users with distinct interest profiles. The content recommended to these users is automatically annotated, enabling systematic statistical analysis across four video categories: containing formal, disclosed, undisclosed and none advertisement; as well as advertisement topical relevance to user's interest. Our findings reveal a stark regulatory paradox. TikTok demonstrates formal compliance with Article 28(2) by shielding minors from profiled formal advertisements, yet both disclosed and undisclosed ads exhibit significant profiling aligned with user interests (5-8 times stronger than for adult formal advertising). The strongest profiling emerges within undisclosed commercial content, where brands/creators fail to label promotional content/paid partnership and the platform neither corrects this omission nor prevents its personalized delivery to minors. We argue that protecting minors requires expanding the regulatory definition of advertisement to encompass brand/influencer marketing and extending the Article 28(2) prohibition accordingly, ensuring that commercial content cannot circumvent protections merely by operating outside formal advertising channels.

zh.title: 数字服务法的盲点：TikTok上广告和未成年人分析的算法审计
zh.summary: 欧盟法规禁止针对未成年人的定向广告，但不包括影响者营销。算法审计显示，TikTok仍然通过未披露的推广内容对未成年人进行分析，暴露了一个关键的监管漏洞。

fr.title: Le Point Aveugle du DSA : Audit Algorithmique de la Publicité et du Profilage des Mineurs sur TikTok
fr.summary: Les réglementations de l'UE interdisent la publicité ciblée aux mineurs mais excluent le marketing d'influence. Un audit algorithmique révèle que TikTok profile toujours les mineurs par le biais de contenu promotionnel non divulgué, exposant une lacune réglementaire critique.

de.title: Der blinde Fleck des DSA: Algorithmische Prüfung von Werbung und Profilierung Minderjähriger auf TikTok
de.summary: EU-Vorschriften verbieten Zielwerbung für Minderjährige, schließen aber Influencer-Marketing aus. Ein algorithmischer Audit zeigt, dass TikTok Minderjährige durch undiskutierte Werbeinhalte weiterhin profiliert, was eine kritische regulatorische Lücke aufdeckt.

es.title: El Punto Ciego del DSA: Auditoría Algorítmica de Publicidad y Perfilado de Menores en TikTok
es.summary: Las regulaciones de la UE prohíben la publicidad dirigida a menores pero excluyen el marketing de influencers. Una auditoría algorítmica revela que TikTok sigue perfilando a menores a través de contenido promocional no divulgado, exponiendo una brecha regulatoria crítica.

title: When Rubrics Fail: Error Enumeration as Reward in Reference-Free RL Post-Training for Virtual Try-On
summary: Researchers propose Implicit Error Counting (IEC) for reinforcement learning reward signals in tasks with multiple valid outputs. IEC outperforms rubric-based approaches on virtual try-on by focusing on error enumeration rather than rubric compliance.
content:
arXiv:2603.05659v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) and Rubrics as Rewards (RaR) have driven strong gains in domains with clear correctness signals and even in subjective domains by synthesizing evaluation criteria from ideal reference answers. But many real-world tasks admit multiple valid outputs and lack the single ideal answer that rubric generation depends on. We identify this reference-free setting as a gap in current post-training methods and propose Implicit Error Counting (IEC) to fill it. Instead of checking what a response gets right against a rubric, IEC enumerates what it gets wrong, applying severity-weighted scores across task-relevant axes and converting them into calibrated per-aspect rewards. We show that na\"ive explicit enumeration is too noisy for stable optimization, and that two design choices: implicit score emission and group calibration are necessary to make error counting a reliable reward. As a case study, we validate IEC on virtual try-on (VTO), a domain that is simultaneously too constrained for holistic scoring and too permissive for rubric-based evaluation: subtle garment errors are unacceptable, yet many output variations are correct. We introduce Cascaded Error Counting (CEC) as an evaluation metric, which tracks human preferences well (60% top-1 vs. 30% others), and curate Mismatch-DressCode (MDressBench), a benchmark with maximal attribute mismatch to stress-test reward designs. On MDressBench, IEC outperforms RaR across all metrics (CEC: 5.31 vs. 5.60 on flat references; 5.20 vs. 5.53 on non-flat). On VITON-HD and DressCode, IEC matches or surpasses six baselines on 6 of 8 perceptual metrics. These results suggest that when ideal answers are unavailable, counting errors provide a stronger signal than constructing rubrics.

zh.title: 当评分标准失效时：虚拟试穿中无参考强化学习后训练的错误枚举奖励
zh.summary: 研究人员为具有多个有效输出的任务提出隐式错误计数（IEC）作为强化学习奖励信号。IEC通过关注错误枚举而不是评分标准合规性，在虚拟试穿上超越了基于评分标准的方法。

fr.title: Quand les Rubriques Échouent : Énumération d'Erreurs comme Récompense dans le Post-Entraînement RL sans Référence pour l'Essayage Virtuel
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent le Comptage Implicite d'Erreurs (IEC) pour les signaux de récompense d'apprentissage par renforcement dans les tâches avec plusieurs sorties valides. L'IEC surpasse les approches basées sur les rubriques dans l'essayage virtuel en se concentrant sur l'énumération d'erreurs plutôt que la conformité aux rubriques.

de.title: Wenn Rubriken Fehlschlagen: Fehleraufzählung als Belohnung in Referenzfreiem RL Post-Training für Virtuelles Anprobieren
de.summary: Forscher schlagen Implizites Fehlerzählen (IEC) für Verstärkungslern-Belohnungssignale in Aufgaben mit mehreren gültigen Ausgaben vor. IEC übertrifft rubrikbasierte Ansätze beim virtuellen Anprobieren, indem es sich auf Fehleraufzählung statt auf Einhaltung von Rubriken konzentriert.

es.title: Cuando las Rúbricas Fallan: Enumeración de Errores como Recompensa en el Post-Entrenamiento RL sin Referencia para Prueba Virtual
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen Conteo Implícito de Errores (IEC) para señales de recompensa de aprendizaje por refuerzo en tareas con múltiples salidas válidas. IEC supera los enfoques basados en rúbricas en la prueba virtual al enfocarse en la enumeración de errores en lugar de cumplimiento de rúbricas.

title: SecureRAG-RTL: A Retrieval-Augmented, Multi-Agent, Zero-Shot LLM-Driven Framework for Hardware Vulnerability Detection
summary: A retrieval-augmented generation framework enables smaller LLMs to detect hardware vulnerabilities despite limited training data. The method improves detection accuracy by approximately 30% across diverse model architectures.
content:
arXiv:2603.05689v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have shown remarkable capabilities in natural language processing tasks, yet their application in hardware security verification remains limited due to scarcity of publicly available hardware description language (HDL) datasets. This knowledge gap constrains LLM performance in detecting vulnerabilities within HDL designs. To address this challenge, we propose SecureRAG-RTL, a novel Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)-based approach that significantly enhances LLM-based security verification of hardware designs. Our approach integrates domain-specific retrieval with generative reasoning, enabling models to overcome inherent limitations in hardware security expertise. We establish baseline vulnerability detection rates using prompt-only methods and then demonstrate that SecureRAG-RTL achieves substantial improvements across diverse LLM architectures, regardless of size. On average, our method increases detection accuracy by about 30%, highlighting its effectiveness in bridging domain knowledge gaps. For evaluation, we curated and annotated a benchmark dataset of 14 HDL designs containing real-world security vulnerabilities, which we will release publicly to support future research. These findings underscore the potential of RAG-driven augmentation to enable scalable, efficient, and accurate hardware security verification workflows.

zh.title: SecureRAG-RTL：用于硬件漏洞检测的检索增强、多智能体、零样本大语言模型驱动框架
zh.summary: 一个检索增强生成框架使得较小的大语言模型能够检测硬件漏洞，尽管训练数据有限。该方法在各种模型架构上的检测准确度提高了约30%。

fr.title: SecureRAG-RTL : Un cadre d'augmentation par récupération, multi-agent et zéro-shot piloté par LLM pour la détection de vulnérabilités matérielles
fr.summary: Un framework de génération augmentée par récupération permet aux petits LLM de détecter les vulnérabilités matérielles malgré les données d'entraînement limitées. La méthode améliore la précision de détection d'environ 30% sur diverses architectures de modèles.

de.title: SecureRAG-RTL: Ein abrufaugmentiertes, Multi-Agent-, Zero-Shot-LLM-gesteuertes Framework zur Erkennung von Hardware-Anfälligkeiten
de.summary: Ein Abruf-augmentiertes Generierungs-Framework ermöglicht es kleineren LLMs, Hardware-Anfälligkeiten trotz begrenzter Trainingsdaten zu erkennen. Die Methode verbessert die Erkennungsgenauigkeit um etwa 30% über verschiedene Modellarchitekturen hinweg.

es.title: SecureRAG-RTL: Un marco impulsado por LLM de recuperación aumentada, multi-agente y zero-shot para la detección de vulnerabilidades de hardware
es.summary: Un marco de generación aumentada por recuperación permite que LLMs más pequeños detecten vulnerabilidades de hardware a pesar de los datos de entrenamiento limitados. El método mejora la precisión de detección en aproximadamente 30% en diversas arquitecturas de modelos.

title: Longitudinal Lesion Inpainting in Brain MRI via 3D Region Aware Diffusion
summary: A diffusion-based framework inpaints evolving brain lesions in longitudinal MRI scans while maintaining 3D anatomical continuity. The approach achieves 10x speedup and superior fidelity compared to existing methods for preprocessing neurodegenerative disease studies.
content:
arXiv:2603.05693v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Accurate longitudinal analysis of brain MRI is often hindered by evolving lesions, which bias automated neuroimaging pipelines. While deep generative models have shown promise in inpainting these lesions, most existing methods operate cross-sectionally or lack 3D anatomical continuity. We present a novel pseudo-3D longitudinal inpainting framework based on Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPM). Our approach utilizes multi-channel conditioning to incorporate longitudinal context from distinct visits (t_1, t_2) and extends Region-Aware Diffusion (RAD) to the medical domain, focusing the generative process on pathological regions without altering surrounding healthy tissue. We evaluated our model against state-of-the-art baselines on longitudinal brain MRI from 93 patients. Our model significantly outperforms the leading baseline (FastSurfer-LIT) in terms of perceptual fidelity, reducing the Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity (LPIPS) distance from 0.07 to 0.03 while effectively eliminating inter-slice discontinuities. Furthermore, our model demonstrates high longitudinal stability with a Temporal Fidelity Index of 1.024, closely approaching the ideal value of 1.0 and substantially narrowing the gap compared to LIT's TFI of 1.22. Notably, the RAD mechanism provides a substantial gain in efficiency; our framework achieves an average processing time of 2.53 min per volume, representing approximately 10x speedup over the 24.30 min required by LIT. By leveraging longitudinal priors and region-specific denoising, our framework provides a highly reliable and efficient preprocessing step for the study of progressive neurodegenerative diseases. A derivative dataset consisting of 93 pre-processed scans used for testing will be available upon request after acceptance. Code will be released upon acceptance.

zh.title: 通过3D区域感知扩散模型进行脑MRI的纵向病灶修复
zh.summary: 一个基于扩散的框架在纵向MRI扫描中修复演变中的脑病灶，同时保持3D解剖学连续性。与现有方法相比，该方法在神经退行性疾病研究的预处理中实现了10倍加速和更好的保真度。

fr.title: Inpainting de lésions longitudinales dans l'IRM cérébrale via diffusion consciente des régions 3D
fr.summary: Un framework basé sur la diffusion repeint les lésions cérébrales évolutives dans les acquisitions IRM longitudinales tout en maintenant la continuité anatomique 3D. L'approche réalise une accélération 10x et une fidélité supérieure par rapport aux méthodes existantes pour le prétraitement des études de maladies neurodégénératives.

de.title: Längslädierter Lesions-Inpainting in Gehirn-MRT durch 3D-regionsbewusste Diffusion
de.summary: Ein Diffusions-basiertes Framework repariert evolvierende Hirnläsionen in longitudinalen MRT-Aufnahmen, während es die 3D-anatomische Kontinuität bewahrt. Der Ansatz erreicht eine 10-fache Beschleunigung und überlegene Wiedergabetreue im Vergleich zu vorhandenen Methoden für die Vorverarbeitung von Studien zur neurodegenerativen Erkrankung.

es.title: Inpainting de lesiones longitudinales en resonancia magnética cerebral mediante difusión consciente de regiones 3D
es.summary: Un marco basado en difusión repara lesiones cerebrales en evolución en resonancias magnéticas longitudinales manteniendo la continuidad anatómica 3D. El enfoque logra una aceleración 10x y una fidelidad superior en comparación con los métodos existentes para el preprocesamiento de estudios de enfermedades neurodegenerativas.

title: Autonomous Algorithm Discovery for Ptychography via Evolutionary LLM Reasoning
summary: An LLM-driven evolutionary framework autonomously discovers regularization algorithms for computational imaging. Discovered algorithms outperform conventional methods on challenging datasets with up to 0.26 SSIM and 8.3 dB PSNR improvements.
content:
arXiv:2603.05696v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Ptychography is a computational imaging technique widely used for high-resolution materials characterization, but high-quality reconstructions often require the use of regularization functions that largely remain manually designed. We introduce Ptychi-Evolve, an autonomous framework that uses large language models (LLMs) to discover and evolve novel regularization algorithms. The framework combines LLM-driven code generation with evolutionary mechanisms, including semantically-guided crossover and mutation. Experiments on three challenging datasets (X-ray integrated circuits, low-dose electron microscopy of apoferritin, and multislice imaging with crosstalk artifacts) demonstrate that discovered regularizers outperform conventional reconstructions, achieving up to +0.26 SSIM and +8.3~dB PSNR improvements. Besides, Ptychi-Evolve records algorithm lineage and evolution metadata, enabling interpretable and reproducible analysis of discovered regularizers.

zh.title: 通过进化型大语言模型推理进行叠层成像自主算法发现
zh.summary: 一个大语言模型驱动的进化框架自主发现计算成像的正则化算法。发现的算法在具有挑战性的数据集上的性能优于传统方法，最高可达0.26 SSIM和8.3 dB PSNR的改进。

fr.title: Découverte autonome d'algorithmes pour la ptychographie via le raisonnement évolutif des LLM
fr.summary: Un framework évolutif piloté par LLM découvre de manière autonome des algorithmes de régularisation pour l'imagerie computationnelle. Les algorithmes découverts surpassent les méthodes conventionnelles sur les ensembles de données difficiles avec des améliorations jusqu'à 0,26 SSIM et 8,3 dB PSNR.

de.title: Autonome Algorithmenerkennung für Ptychographie durch evolutionäres LLM-Reasoning
de.summary: Ein LLM-gesteuertes evolutionäres Framework entdeckt autonom Regularisierungsalgorithmen für rechnergestützte Bildgebung. Entdeckte Algorithmen übertreffen konventionelle Methoden auf schwierigen Datensätzen mit Verbesserungen von bis zu 0,26 SSIM und 8,3 dB PSNR.

es.title: Descubrimiento autónomo de algoritmos para ptycografía mediante razonamiento evolutivo de LLM
es.summary: Un marco evolutivo impulsado por LLM descubre de forma autónoma algoritmos de regularización para imágenes computacionales. Los algoritmos descubiertos superan los métodos convencionales en conjuntos de datos desafiantes con mejoras de hasta 0,26 SSIM y 8,3 dB PSNR.

title: The Rise of AI in Weather and Climate Information and its Impact on Global Inequality
summary: Analysis reveals AI development in climate modeling is concentrated in the Global North, perpetuating inequalities through biased training data and sparse coverage. This systemic bias risks performance gaps in climate information for the most vulnerable regions.
content:
arXiv:2603.05710v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The rapid adoption of AI in Earth system science promises unprecedented speed and fidelity in the generation of climate information. However, this technological prowess rests on a fragile and unequal foundation: the current trajectory of AI development risks further automating and amplifying the North-South divide in the global climate information system. We outline the global asymmetry in High-Performance Computing and data infrastructure, demonstrating that the development of foundation models is almost exclusively concentrated in the Global North. Using three different domains, we show how this infrastructure inequality continues through models' inputs, processes and outputs. As an example, in weather and climate modelling, the reliance on historically biased data leads to systematic performance gaps that disproportionately affect the most vulnerable regions. In climate impact modelling, data sparsity and unrepresentative validation risk driving misleading interventions and maladaptation. Finally, in large language models, dependence on dominant textualised forms of climate knowledge risks reinforcing existing biases. We conclude that addressing these disparities demands revisiting the three phases, i.e. models Input, Process and Output. This involves (i) a perspective shift from model-centric to data-centric development, (ii) the establishment of a Climate Digital Public Infrastructure and human-centric evaluation metrics, and (iii) a move from producer-consumer dynamics toward knowledge co-production. This integration of diverse knowledge systems would truly democratise compute sovereignty and ensure that the AI revolution fosters genuine systemic resilience rather than exacerbating inequity.

zh.title: 人工智能在天气和气候信息中的崛起及其对全球不平等的影响
zh.summary: 分析表明气候建模中的人工智能发展集中在全球北方，通过有偏见的训练数据和稀疏覆盖加剧不平等。这种系统性偏差为最脆弱地区的气候信息带来性能差距的风险。

fr.title: L'essor de l'IA dans la prévision météorologique et l'information climatique et son impact sur les inégalités mondiales
fr.summary: L'analyse révèle que le développement de l'IA en modélisation climatique est concentré dans le Nord global, perpétuant les inégalités par des données d'entraînement biaisées et une couverture clairsemée. Ce biais systémique risque des écarts de performance dans l'information climatique pour les régions les plus vulnérables.

de.title: Der Aufstieg der KI in der Wetter- und Klimainformation und ihre Auswirkungen auf globale Ungleichheit
de.summary: Die Analyse zeigt, dass die KI-Entwicklung in der Klimamodellierung im Globalen Norden konzentriert ist und Ungleichheiten durch verzerrte Trainingsdaten und spärliche Abdeckung perpetuiert. Diese systematische Verzerrung birgt das Risiko von Leistungslücken in der Klimainformation für die am meisten gefährdeten Regionen.

es.title: El auge de la IA en la información meteorológica y climática y su impacto en la desigualdad global
es.summary: El análisis revela que el desarrollo de la IA en modelado climático se concentra en el Norte global, perpetuando desigualdades a través de datos de entrenamiento sesgados y cobertura escasa. Este sesgo sistemático presenta un riesgo de brechas de desempeño en la información climática para las regiones más vulnerables.

title: Cultural Perspectives and Expectations for Generative AI: A Global Survey Approach
summary: A large-scale global survey identifies diverse cultural expectations for generative AI representations. The research recommends participatory development approaches and sensitivity frameworks to address cultural dimensions beyond geographic boundaries.
content:
arXiv:2603.05723v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: There is a lack of empirical evidence about global attitudes around whether and how GenAI should represent cultures. This paper assesses understandings and beliefs about culture as it relates to GenAI from a large-scale global survey. We gathered data about what culture means to different groups, and about how GenAI should approach the representation of cultural artifacts, concepts, or values. We distill working definitions of culture directly from these communities to build an understanding of its conceptual complexities and how they relate to representations in Generative AI. We survey from across parts of Europe, North and South America, Asia, and Africa. We conclude with a set of recommendations for Culture and GenAI development. These include participatory approaches, prioritizing specific cultural dimensions beyond geography, such as religion and tradition, and a sensitivity framework for addressing cultural ``redlines''.

zh.title: 生成式人工智能的文化观点和期望：全球调查方法
zh.summary: 一项大规模全球调查确定了对生成式人工智能表现的多样化文化期望。该研究建议采用参与式开发方法和敏感性框架来解决超越地理边界的文化维度。

fr.title: Perspectives culturelles et attentes envers l'IA générative : une approche d'enquête mondiale
fr.summary: Un sondage mondial à grande échelle identifie des attentes culturelles diverses envers les représentations de l'IA générative. La recherche recommande des approches de développement participatif et des cadres de sensibilité pour aborder les dimensions culturelles au-delà des frontières géographiques.

de.title: Kulturelle Perspektiven und Erwartungen an generative KI: Ein globaler Umfrage-Ansatz
de.summary: Eine großangelegte globale Umfrage identifiziert vielfältige kulturelle Erwartungen an generative KI-Darstellungen. Die Forschung empfiehlt partizipative Entwicklungsansätze und Sensibilitätsrahmen, um kulturelle Dimensionen über geografische Grenzen hinweg zu adressieren.

es.title: Perspectivas culturales y expectativas para la IA generativa: un enfoque de encuesta global
es.summary: Una encuesta global a gran escala identifica expectativas culturales diversas para las representaciones de IA generativa. La investigación recomienda enfoques de desarrollo participativo y marcos de sensibilidad para abordar dimensiones culturales más allá de las fronteras geográficas.

title: LTLGuard: Formalizing LTL Specifications with Compact Language Models and Lightweight Symbolic Reasoning
summary: A framework enables resource-efficient small language models (4B–14B parameters) to generate correct linear temporal logic specifications from natural language requirements. It combines constrained generation with formal consistency checking to eliminate invalid outputs.
content:
arXiv:2603.05728v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Translating informal requirements into formal specifications is challenging due to the ambiguity and variability of natural language (NL). This challenge is particularly pronounced when relying on compact (small and medium) language models, which may lack robust knowledge of temporal logic and thus struggle to produce syntactically valid and consistent formal specifications. In this work, we focus on enabling resource-efficient open-weight models (4B--14B parameters) to generate correct linear temporal logic (LTL) specifications from informal requirements. We present LTLGuard, a modular toolchain that combines constrained generation with formal consistency checking to generate conflict-free LTL specifications from informal input. Our method integrates the generative capabilities of model languages with lightweight automated reasoning tools to iteratively refine candidate specifications, understand the origin of the conflicts and thus help in eliminating inconsistencies. We demonstrate the usability and the effectiveness of our approach and perform quantitative evaluation of the resulting framework.

zh.title: LTLGuard：使用紧凑语言模型和轻量级符号推理形式化LTL规范
zh.summary: 一个框架使资源高效的小型语言模型（4B–14B参数）能够从自然语言需求生成正确的线性时间逻辑规范。它将受限生成与形式一致性检查相结合，以消除无效输出。

fr.title: LTLGuard : Formalisation des spécifications LTL avec des modèles de langage compacts et un raisonnement symbolique léger
fr.summary: Un cadre permet à des modèles de langage petits et efficaces en ressources (4B–14B paramètres) de générer des spécifications de logique temporelle linéaire correctes à partir des exigences du langage naturel. Il combine la génération contrainte avec la vérification de cohérence formelle pour éliminer les sorties invalides.

de.title: LTLGuard: Formalisierung von LTL-Spezifikationen mit kompakten Sprachmodellen und leichtem symbolischen Reasoning
de.summary: Ein Framework ermöglicht ressourceneffizienten kleinen Sprachmodellen (4B–14B Parameter) die Generierung korrekter linearer Temporallogik-Spezifikationen aus natürlichsprachlichen Anforderungen. Es kombiniert eingeschränkte Generierung mit formaler Konsistenzprüfung, um ungültige Ausgaben zu eliminieren.

es.title: LTLGuard: Formalización de especificaciones LTL con modelos de lenguaje compactos y razonamiento simbólico ligero
es.summary: Un marco permite que modelos de lenguaje pequeños y eficientes en recursos (4B–14B parámetros) generen especificaciones correctas de lógica temporal lineal a partir de requisitos en lenguaje natural. Combina generación restringida con verificación de coherencia formal para eliminar salidas inválidas.

title: Revisiting the (Sub)Optimality of Best-of-N for Inference-Time Alignment
summary: Theoretical analysis demonstrates Best-of-N sampling is statistically optimal for win-rate based inference-time alignment. The research proposes a simple variant that maintains optimality while eliminating vulnerability to reward hacking.
content:
arXiv:2603.05739v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Best-of-N (BoN) sampling is a widely used inference-time alignment method for language models, whereby N candidate responses are sampled from a reference model and the one with the highest predicted reward according to a learned reward model is selected. Despite its widespread practical use, recent theoretical work has suggested that it is statistically suboptimal and vulnerable to reward hacking, the process by which models exploit weaknesses in the learned reward model to achieve high estimated reward without genuinely improving performance. We revisit this question under assumptions that more closely reflect practice than that of prior work. In particular, in contradistinction to earlier analyses that focused on expected true reward, which may not be meaningful in many practical settings, we investigate how inference-time alignment affects the win-rate, a pairwise comparison-based metric more closely aligned with how reward models are trained and evaluated in practice. We demonstrate that, under minimal conditions on the quality of the reference model and learned reward model, properly tuned BoN is both computationally and statistically optimal in achieving high win-rate, partially explaining its widespread practical success. Because BoN remains susceptible to reward-hacking in this setting, we propose a simple and practical variant that provably eliminates reward-hacking while maintaining optimal statistical performance. Finally, we show that prior approaches are provably suboptimal when considering win-rate, highlighting the importance of choosing appropriate objectives when analyzing inference-time alignment methods.

zh.title: 重新审视最优-N推理时对齐的(次)最优性
zh.summary: 理论分析表明,基于胜率的最优-N采样在推理时对齐中在统计学上是最优的。该研究提出了一个简单的变体,既保持最优性,又消除了对奖励黑客攻击的脆弱性。

fr.title: Réexamen de la (sous-)optimalité du meilleur-de-N pour l'alignement au moment de l'inférence
fr.summary: L'analyse théorique démontre que l'échantillonnage du meilleur-de-N est statistiquement optimal pour l'alignement au moment de l'inférence basé sur le taux de victoire. La recherche propose une variante simple qui maintient l'optimalité tout en éliminant la vulnérabilité au piratage des récompenses.

de.title: Überprüfung der (Sub-)Optimalität von Best-of-N für das Alignment zur Inferenzzeit
de.summary: Die theoretische Analyse zeigt, dass Best-of-N-Sampling für das Alignment zur Inferenzzeit basierend auf der Gewinnrate statistisch optimal ist. Die Forschung schlägt eine einfache Variante vor, die die Optimalität beibehält und gleichzeitig die Anfälligkeit für Reward-Hacking beseitigt.

es.title: Revisitando la (sub)optimalidad de Best-of-N para alineación en tiempo de inferencia
es.summary: El análisis teórico demuestra que el muestreo Best-of-N es estadísticamente óptimo para la alineación en tiempo de inferencia basada en la tasa de victorias. La investigación propone una variante simple que mantiene la optimalidad mientras elimina la vulnerabilidad al pirateo de recompensas.

title: TML-Bench: Benchmark for Data Science Agents on Tabular ML Tasks
summary: A new benchmark evaluates autonomous coding agents on Kaggle-style tabular machine learning tasks with strict time constraints. Tests of 10 open-source LLMs show performance varies significantly with budget, providing systematic evaluation for future agent development.
content:
arXiv:2603.05764v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Autonomous coding agents can produce strong tabular baselines quickly on Kaggle-style tasks. Practical value depends on end-to-end correctness and reliability under time limits. This paper introduces TML-Bench, a tabular benchmark for data science agents on Kaggle-style tasks. This paper evaluates 10 OSS LLMs on four Kaggle competitions and three time budgets (240s, 600s, and 1200s). Each model is run five times per task and budget. A run is successful if it produces a valid submission and a private-holdout score on hidden labels that are not accessible to the agent. This paper reports median performance, success rates, and run-to-run variability. MiniMax-M2.1 model achieves the best aggregate performance score on all four competitions under the paper's primary aggregation. Average performance improves with larger time budgets. Scaling is noisy for some individual models at the current run count. Code and materials are available at https://github.com/MykolaPinchuk/TML-bench/tree/master.

zh.title: TML-Bench:表格机器学习任务中数据科学智能体的基准测试
zh.summary: 一个新的基准测试评估了自主编码智能体在具有严格时间限制的Kaggle风格表格机器学习任务上的性能。对10个开源大型语言模型的测试表明,性能随预算显著变化,为未来智能体开发提供了系统的评估。

fr.title: TML-Bench: Benchmark pour les agents de science des données sur les tâches de ML tabulaires
fr.summary: Un nouveau benchmark évalue les agents de codage autonomes sur des tâches d'apprentissage automatique tabulaire de style Kaggle avec des contraintes de temps strictes. Les tests de 10 LLM open-source montrent que les performances varient considérablement selon le budget, ce qui fournit une évaluation systématique pour le développement futur des agents.

de.title: TML-Bench: Benchmark für Data-Science-Agenten bei tabellarischen ML-Aufgaben
de.summary: Ein neuer Benchmark evaluiert autonome Kodieragenten bei Kaggle-ähnlichen tabellarischen Machine-Learning-Aufgaben mit strikten Zeitbeschränkungen. Tests von 10 Open-Source-LLMs zeigen, dass die Leistung stark mit dem Budget variiert und bieten eine systematische Bewertung für die zukünftige Agententwicklung.

es.title: TML-Bench: Benchmark para agentes de ciencia de datos en tareas de ML tabular
es.summary: Un nuevo benchmark evalúa agentes de codificación autónomos en tareas de aprendizaje automático tabulares de estilo Kaggle con restricciones de tiempo estrictas. Las pruebas de 10 LLM de código abierto muestran que el rendimiento varía significativamente con el presupuesto, proporcionando una evaluación sistemática para el desarrollo futuro de agentes.

title: Bridging Domains through Subspace-Aware Model Merging
summary: The SCORE method resolves subspace conflicts when merging models trained on different domains by projecting into shared orthogonal bases. It consistently outperforms existing approaches in domain generalization across diverse architectures and model scales.
content:
arXiv:2603.05768v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Model merging integrates multiple task-specific models into a single consolidated one. Recent research has made progress in improving merging performance for in-distribution or multi-task scenarios, but domain generalization in model merging remains underexplored. We investigate how merging models fine-tuned on distinct domains affects generalization to unseen domains. Through an analysis of parameter competition in the task matrix using singular value decomposition, we show that merging models trained under different distribution shifts induces stronger conflicts between their subspaces compared to traditional multi-task settings. To mitigate this issue, we propose SCORE (Subspace COnflict-Resolving mErging), a method designed to alleviate such singular subspace conflicts. SCORE finds a shared orthogonal basis by computing the principal components of the concatenated leading singular vectors of all models. It then projects each task matrix into the shared basis, pruning off-diagonal components to remove conflicting singular directions. SCORE consistently outperforms, on average, existing model merging approaches in domain generalization settings across a variety of architectures and model scales, demonstrating its effectiveness and scalability.

zh.title: 通过子空间感知模型合并连接领域
zh.summary: SCORE方法通过投影到共享的正交基来解决在合并在不同领域上训练的模型时出现的子空间冲突。它在各种架构和模型规模的域泛化中始终优于现有方法。

fr.title: Relier les domaines par la fusion de modèles conscients de sous-espaces
fr.summary: La méthode SCORE résout les conflits de sous-espace lors de la fusion de modèles entraînés sur différents domaines en projetant dans des bases orthogonales partagées. Elle surpasse systématiquement les approches existantes en généralisation de domaines dans diverses architectures et échelles de modèles.

de.title: Domänen durch Subspace-bewusstes Modell-Merging verbinden
de.summary: Die SCORE-Methode löst Subspace-Konflikte beim Zusammenführen von Modellen, die auf verschiedenen Domänen trainiert wurden, durch Projektion in gemeinsame orthogonale Basen. Sie übertrifft konsistent bestehende Ansätze bei der Domänengeneralisierung über verschiedene Architekturen und Modellskalen hinweg.

es.title: Conectando dominios a través de la fusión de modelos conscientes del subespacio
es.summary: El método SCORE resuelve conflictos de subespacio al fusionar modelos entrenados en diferentes dominios proyectando en bases ortogonales compartidas. Consistentemente supera a los enfoques existentes en la generalización del dominio en diversas arquitecturas y escalas de modelos.

title: Depth Charge: Jailbreak Large Language Models from Deep Safety Attention Heads
summary: The Safety Attention Head Attack (SAHA) targets deeper attention layers to jailbreak aligned LLMs, achieving 14% improvement in attack success rate. It reveals previously unexploited vulnerabilities in safety mechanisms that previous shallow-level attacks missed.
content:
arXiv:2603.05772v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Currently, open-sourced large language models (OSLLMs) have demonstrated remarkable generative performance. However, as their structure and weights are made public, they are exposed to jailbreak attacks even after alignment. Existing attacks operate primarily at shallow levels, such as the prompt or embedding level, and often fail to expose vulnerabilities rooted in deeper model components, which creates a false sense of security for successful defense. In this paper, we propose \textbf{\underline{S}}afety \textbf{\underline{A}}ttention \textbf{\underline{H}}ead \textbf{\underline{A}}ttack (\textbf{SAHA}), an attention-head-level jailbreak framework that explores the vulnerability in deeper but insufficiently aligned attention heads. SAHA contains two novel designs. Firstly, we reveal that deeper attention layers introduce more vulnerability against jailbreak attacks. Based on this finding, \textbf{SAHA} introduces \textit{Ablation-Impact Ranking} head selection strategy to effectively locate the most vital layer for unsafe output. Secondly, we introduce a boundary-aware perturbation method, \textit{i.e. Layer-Wise Perturbation}, to probe the generation of unsafe content with minimal perturbation to the attention. This constrained perturbation guarantees higher semantic relevance with the target intent while ensuring evasion. Extensive experiments show the superiority of our method: SAHA improves ASR by 14\% over SOTA baselines, revealing the vulnerability of the attack surface on the attention head. Our code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/SAHA.

zh.title: 深度炸弹：从深层安全注意力头越狱大型语言模型
zh.summary: 安全注意力头攻击（SAHA）针对更深层的注意力层来越狱对齐的大型语言模型，实现了14%的攻击成功率改进。它揭示了先前的浅层攻击未发现的安全机制中先前未被利用的漏洞。

fr.title: Charge profonde : Jailbreak des grands modèles de langage à partir des têtes d'attention de sécurité profondes
fr.summary: L'attaque Safety Attention Head (SAHA) cible les couches d'attention plus profondes pour jailbreaker les LLM alignés, réalisant une amélioration de 14% du taux de succès des attaques. Elle révèle des vulnérabilités précédemment inexploitées dans les mécanismes de sécurité que les attaques de niveau peu profond précédentes ont manquées.

de.title: Tiefenladeung: Jailbreak großer Sprachmodelle aus tiefen Sicherheitsaufmerksamkeitsköpfen
de.summary: Der Safety Attention Head Attack (SAHA) zielt auf tiefere Aufmerksamkeitsschichten ab, um ausgerichtete LLMs zu jailbreaken und erreicht eine 14%ige Verbesserung der Angriffserfolgungsrate. Es offenbart zuvor unerforschte Sicherheitslücken in Sicherheitsmechanismen, die frühere oberflächliche Angriffe übersehen haben.

es.title: Carga Profunda: Jailbreak de Grandes Modelos de Lenguaje desde Cabezas de Atención de Seguridad Profunda
es.summary: El ataque de cabeza de atención de seguridad (SAHA) apunta a capas de atención más profundas para liberar LLM alineados, logrando una mejora del 14% en la tasa de éxito del ataque. Revela vulnerabilidades previamente inexplotadas en mecanismos de seguridad que los ataques de nivel superficial anteriores no detectaron.

title: Knowing without Acting: The Disentangled Geometry of Safety Mechanisms in Large Language Models
summary: The Disentangled Safety Hypothesis reveals that safety computation operates on separate recognition and execution subspaces. The Refusal Erasure Attack demonstrates mechanistic decoupling, achieving state-of-the-art jailbreak performance by surgically targeting refusal mechanisms.
content:
arXiv:2603.05773v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Safety alignment is often conceptualized as a monolithic process wherein harmfulness detection automatically triggers refusal. However, the persistence of jailbreak attacks suggests a fundamental mechanistic decoupling. We propose the \textbf{\underline{D}}isentangled \textbf{\underline{S}}afety \textbf{\underline{H}}ypothesis \textbf{(DSH)}, positing that safety computation operates on two distinct subspaces: a \textit{Recognition Axis} ($\mathbf{v}_H$, ``Knowing'') and an \textit{Execution Axis} ($\mathbf{v}_R$, ``Acting''). Our geometric analysis reveals a universal ``Reflex-to-Dissociation'' evolution, where these signals transition from antagonistic entanglement in early layers to structural independence in deep layers. To validate this, we introduce \textit{Double-Difference Extraction} and \textit{Adaptive Causal Steering}. Using our curated \textsc{AmbiguityBench}, we demonstrate a causal double dissociation, effectively creating a state of ``Knowing without Acting.'' Crucially, we leverage this disentanglement to propose the \textbf{Refusal Erasure Attack (REA)}, which achieves State-of-the-Art attack success rates by surgically lobotomizing the refusal mechanism. Furthermore, we uncover a critical architectural divergence, contrasting the \textit{Explicit Semantic Control} of Llama3.1 with the \textit{Latent Distributed Control} of Qwen2.5. The code and dataset are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/DSH.

zh.title: 不行动而知：大型语言模型安全机制的解缠几何
zh.summary: 解缠安全假说揭示了安全计算在独立的识别和执行子空间中进行。拒绝擦除攻击演示了机制解缠，通过外科手术方式针对拒绝机制来实现最先进的越狱性能。

fr.title: Savoir sans agir : La géométrie démêlée des mécanismes de sécurité dans les grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: L'hypothèse de sécurité démêlée révèle que le calcul de sécurité fonctionne sur des sous-espaces de reconnaissance et d'exécution séparés. L'attaque par effacement de refus démontre le découplage mécaniste, réalisant les performances de jailbreak de pointe en ciblant chirurgicalement les mécanismes de refus.

de.title: Wissen ohne Handeln: Die entwirrte Geometrie der Sicherheitsmechanismen in großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Die Disentangled-Sicherheitshypothese offenbart, dass Sicherheitsberechnung in separaten Erkennungs- und Ausführungsunterbereichen funktioniert. Der Refusal-Erasure-Angriff zeigt mechanistische Entkopplung und erreicht modernste Jailbreak-Leistung durch chirurgisches Ansprechen von Verweigerungsmechanismen.

es.title: Saber sin actuar: La geometría desacoplada de mecanismos de seguridad en grandes modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: La hipótesis de seguridad desacoplada revela que el cálculo de seguridad funciona en subespacios de reconocimiento y ejecución separados. El ataque de borrado de negación demuestra el desacoplamiento mecanicista, logrando un rendimiento de jailbreak de última generación al apuntar quirúrgicamente a mecanismos de negación.

title: PVminerLLM: Structured Extraction of Patient Voice from Patient-Generated Text using Large Language Models
summary: A fine-tuned LLM framework extracts patient voice signals from clinical text with 80–87% F1 performance. It demonstrates reliable patient-centered data extraction is achievable even with smaller models, enabling scalable health equity research.
content:
arXiv:2603.05776v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Motivation: Patient-generated text contains critical information about patients' lived experiences, social circumstances, and engagement in care, including factors that strongly influence adherence, care coordination, and health equity. However, these patient voice signals are rarely available in structured form, limiting their use in patient-centered outcomes research and clinical quality improvement. Reliable extraction of such information is therefore essential for understanding and addressing non-clinical drivers of health outcomes at scale.
  Results: We introduce PVminer, a benchmark for structured extraction of patient voice, and propose PVminerLLM, a supervised fine-tuned large language model tailored to this task. Across multiple datasets and model sizes, PVminerLLM substantially outperforms prompt-based baselines, achieving up to 83.82% F1 for Code prediction, 80.74% F1 for Sub-code prediction, and 87.03% F1 for evidence Span extraction. Notably, strong performance is achieved even with smaller models, demonstrating that reliable patient voice extraction is feasible without extreme model scale. These results enable scalable analysis of social and experiential signals embedded in patient-generated text.
  Availability and Implementation: Code, evaluation scripts, and trained LLMs will be released publicly. Annotated datasets will be made available upon request for research use.
  Keywords: Large Language Models, Supervised Fine-Tuning, Medical Annotation, Patient-Generated Text, Clinical NLP

zh.title: PVminerLLM：使用大型语言模型从患者生成的文本中结构化提取患者声音
zh.summary: 一个微调的大型语言模型框架从临床文本中提取患者声音信号，F1性能为80-87%。它表明即使使用较小的模型，可靠的以患者为中心的数据提取也是可以实现的，这使得可扩展的健康公平研究成为可能。

fr.title: PVminerLLM : Extraction structurée de la voix des patients à partir de textes générés par les patients à l'aide de grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: Un framework LLM affiné extrait les signaux de voix des patients à partir de textes cliniques avec une performance F1 de 80-87%. Il démontre que l'extraction de données centrée sur le patient fiable est réalisable même avec des modèles plus petits, permettant une recherche sur l'équité sanitaire évolutive.

de.title: PVminerLLM: Strukturierte Extraktion von Patientenstimmen aus patientenerzeugtem Text mithilfe großer Sprachmodelle
de.summary: Ein optimiertes LLM-Framework extrahiert Patientenstimmensignale aus klinischem Text mit einer F1-Leistung von 80–87%. Es zeigt, dass zuverlässige patientenzentrierte Datenextraktion auch mit kleineren Modellen erreichbar ist, was skalierbare Gesundheitsgerechtigkeitsforschung ermöglicht.

es.title: PVminerLLM: Extracción estructurada de la voz del paciente a partir de texto generado por el paciente utilizando grandes modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: Un framework LLM ajustado extrae señales de voz del paciente del texto clínico con un rendimiento F1 del 80–87%. Demuestra que la extracción de datos centrada en el paciente confiable es alcanzable incluso con modelos más pequeños, lo que permite la investigación de equidad sanitaria escalable.
